urn:contentItem:6JHC-YV13-SHFW-V51C-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Bristow Group Inc reports 29% sequential fall in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 09:42 EDT Release Date: May 06, 20262026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Bristow Group Inc reports 29% sequential fall in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 09:42 EDT Release Date: May 06, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Bristow Group Inc (NYSE:VTOL), announced net profit of $13.1m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 29% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and down 52% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025]. Earnings per share (EPS) were down 28.6% sequentially from 63.0c in Q4/2025 to 45.0c in Q1/2026.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Total revenue was up 10.9% from $350.5m to $388.7m

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit slumped 52.1% from $27.4m to $13.1m

- EPS slumped 52.6% from 95.0c to 45.0c

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 10.7% to 4.3%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 10.7% to 4.3%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 7.8% to 3.4%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 52.6%, Revenue was up 10.9% and Net Profit was down 52.1%.

ACTIVITIES:

Bristow Group Inc. is a global leader in aviation services, specializing in helicopter transportation, search and rescue, and logistics support for the offshore energy sector. Headquartered in Houston, Texas, the company operates a fleet of advanced helicopters to transport personnel and equipment to remote oil and gas platforms, while also providing emergency medical services and government contract operations. Its core activities emphasize safety, reliability, and efficiency in challenging environments, serving clients across North America, Europe, and Africa. Among its fastest-growing segments are services supporting renewable energy initiatives, such as offshore wind farm installations and maintenance, driven by the global shift toward sustainable energy sources. Additionally, Bristow is expanding its unmanned aircraft systems and digital solutions, enhancing operational capabilities and market reach. This strategic focus positions the company for sustained growth in a dynamic industry.

MARKET POSITION:

Bristow Group Inc. New (NYSE:VTOL), the NYSE's 8th largest Transportation Services company by market cap, soared $19.34 (or 65.4%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $48.90. This means the stock has outperformed 83% of NYSE-listed stocks in the same period. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price increase of 37.8%. Average daily volume of 192,936 shares in the trailing year was 1.5 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JH6-9SW3-SH32-B432-00000-00Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Energy Division Surpasses Auto Sales in Australia2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisTesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Energy Division Surpasses Auto Sales in Australia

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

04 May 2026 11:45 EDT

Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Posts year ended 31 December 2025 results showing its energy generation and storage unit has overtaken the automotive business as the primary revenue driver in Australia. Total revenue declined 11% to AUD 4.5 billion, while net profit after tax fell 19% to AUD 52.7 million. The energy generation and storage portfolio, including residential Powerwall and industrial Megapacks, generated AUD 2.5 billion, surpassing vehicle sales of AUD 1.92 billion. Tesla's Australian automotive revenue declined 25% to 28,856 units, while BYD deliveries reached 52,000, highlighting intensified competition in the Australian EV market. Tesla secured a AUD 3 million regulatory credit under the federal vehicle efficiency scheme, helping offset some of the automotive weakness. Analysts say the dual focus on energy and automotive segments may cushion the downturn and inform its long-term strategy

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

Past Week: Bristow Group Inc. New (NYSE:VTOL) in bottom 2% performers of NYSE market in past week

Bristow Group Inc. New (NYSE:VTOL $43.16), the NYSE's 8th largest Transportation services company by market cap, has flirted with correction, down $5.87 (or 12.0%) in the past week to close at $43.16.

This makes it the thirty-second lowest performer in the NYSE market.

In the past week the market cap has plunged by $170.2 million to $1.3 billion.

Activities

Bristow Group Inc. is a global leader in aviation services, specializing in helicopter transportation, search and rescue, and logistics support for the offshore energy sector. Headquartered in Houston, Texas, the company operates a fleet of advanced helicopters to transport personnel and equipment to remote oil and gas platforms, while also providing emergency medical services and government contract operations. Its core activities emphasize safety, reliability, and efficiency in challenging environments, serving clients across North America, Europe, and Africa. Among its fastest-growing segments are services supporting renewable energy initiatives, such as offshore wind farm installations and maintenance, driven by the global shift toward sustainable energy sources. Additionally, Bristow is expanding its unmanned aircraft systems and digital solutions, enhancing operational capabilities and market reach. This strategic focus positions the company for sustained growth in a dynamic industry. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Transportation services company by market capitalisation.

+ United Parcel Service, Inc. (UPS: $99.88) Market Cap $74 billion, Transportation services sector, P/E: 15.2, PV$1000 (20 yrs): $2,514

Past Week: United Parcel Service, Inc. (NYSE:UPS) in bottom 5% performers of S&P 500 Index in past week

United Parcel Service, Inc. (NYSE:UPS $99.88), the NYSE's 2nd largest Transportation services company by market cap, has slid $6.73 (or 6.3%) in the past week to close at $99.88.

This makes it the nineteenth lowest performer in the S&P 500 Index which was up 3.2% in the past week; a relative price change of -9.5%.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $5 billion to $73.8 billion.

Activities

United Parcel Service, Inc. (UPS), a New York Stock Exchange-listed company, is a global leader in logistics and package delivery, specializing in efficient transportation and supply chain management. Founded in 1907, UPS offers a comprehensive range of services, including domestic and international shipping, freight forwarding, and advanced warehousing solutions, all supported by cutting-edge technology for real-time tracking and optimization. The company's core business centers on reliable delivery networks that connect over 200 countries, facilitating seamless movement of goods for businesses and consumers alike. Among its fastest-growing segments are e-commerce logistics, fueled by the rapid expansion of online retail, and healthcare supply chain services, which address the rising demand for specialized, temperature-controlled transport of pharmaceuticals and medical supplies. Additionally, UPS is accelerating growth in sustainable operations, such as electric vehicle fleets and alternative fuel initiatives, to capitalize on environmental trends and regulatory shifts, positioning the company for continued global expansion. It is the NYSE's 2nd largest Transportation services company by market capitalisation.

United Parcel Service, Inc. (NYSE:UPS) is an industry leader in logistics and transportation, globally recognized for its comprehensive suite of services. Operating a vast network, UPS package delivery is augmented by UPS tracking, ensuring timely service across UPS Ground, UPS Next Day Air, and UPS Worldwide Express. Specializing in both domestic and international needs, the company offers UPS Freight and UPS Supply Chain Solutions, bolstered by UPS healthcare logistics expertise. In catering to modern commerce, UPS e-commerce fulfillment and UPS returns management are pivotal. Convenient customer touchpoints include UPS Store locations and UPS Access Point, enhancing service accessibility. Logistics efficiency is ensured by competitive UPS shipping rates. Meanwhile, UPS international shipping addresses cross-border demands. Strategic agreements in the UPS Teamsters deal and Teamsters UPS contract underscore critical aspects of UPS union contract 2023 negotiations, including UPS labor agreement 2028. Topics like UPS severance limits, UPS employee severance, UPS seniority rules, and UPS workforce seniority define their employment terms amidst ongoing UPS contract updates. </

Past 2 Weeks: XPO, Inc. (NYSE:XPO) falls for a second consecutive week, a two-week fall of 4.3%

XPO, Inc. (NYSE:XPO $211.58), the NYSE's 4th largest Transportation services company by market cap, has fallen $5.13 (or 2.4%) to close at $211.58. The stock fell for a second consecutive week on Wednesday bringing its two-week fall to $9.54 or 4.3%.

In the past two weeks the market cap has declined by $1.1 billion to $24.8 billion.

Activities

XPO Logistics, Inc. (formerly XPO, Inc.), a prominent player on the New York Stock Exchange, is a global leader in transportation and logistics services. The company specializes in a diverse array of offerings, including less-than-truckload (LTL) shipping, truckload transportation, managed logistics, expedited services, and global freight forwarding, serving industries such as manufacturing, retail, and e-commerce. By leveraging an extensive network and advanced technology platforms, XPO optimizes supply chain efficiency for its customers worldwide.

The fastest-growing segments of XPO''s business include its LTL operations, which have expanded rapidly due to investments in digital tools, fleet modernization, and strategic acquisitions. This segment benefits from rising demand for flexible, cost-effective shipping solutions, particularly in the e-commerce sector, where XPO''s focus on last-mile delivery and integrated services has driven significant market share gains. Overall, XPO''s commitment to innovation and operational excellence positions it as a key enabler in the evolving logistics landscape. It is the NYSE's 4th largest Transportation services company by market capitalisation.

XPO, Inc. (NYSE:XPO) is a leading provider in the logistics sector, with a specialized focus on last-mile logistics and a robust presence in Ireland. The company's notable offerings include XPO Logistics last mile delivery solutions tailored for diverse sectors, from B2B retail distribution in Ireland to e-commerce last mile solutions and sustainable last mile delivery. XPO's strategic advantage lies in its comprehensive suite of services—ranging from bulky goods home delivery and two-person delivery service to home delivery assembly service and installation and assembly services. With an emphasis on cutting-edge last mile logistics technology, including route optimization and tracking, XPO excels in real-time delivery tracking and contactless home delivery. As a trusted logistics partner for B&Q, XPO provides a dedicated last mile carrier solution, enhancing its market position with reliable furniture delivery across Ireland. By leveraging white glove delivery Ireland options and omni-channel fulfillment Ireland, XPO propels significant growth potential in the logistics domain.

urn:contentItem:6JHM-RY53-RV86-D562-00000-00Subsea7 (OT:SUBCY) Secures Offshore Angola Contract, Expands Global Footprint2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisSubsea7 (OT:SUBCY) Secures Offshore Angola Contract, Expands Global Footprint

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 14:17 EDT

Subsea7 (OT:SUBCY (ADR)) has secured a contract offshore Angola, with the award announced on 6th of May 2026, signaling a strategic expansion in West Africa's energy landscape. Project management and engineering will be directed from Subsea7 offices in Paris, Luanda, Lisbon, and Sutton, while the umbilical scope will be executed by SLB OneSubsea, a member of the Subsea Integration Alliance, from its Center of Excellence in Moss, Norway, with additional support from Houston-based teams. David Bertin, Senior Vice President for Global Projects Center East at Subsea7, states, "This project builds on our track record in West Africa, Australia, and the US. Together with ExxonMobil, we are committed to delivering the project safely, efficiently, and to the highest standards, while continuing to support the development of local capabilities in Angola." Olivier Blaringhem, CEO of Subsea Integration Alliance, notes, "This award further strengthens our relationship with ExxonMobil. It demonstrates how early collaboration through Subsea Integration Alliance enables an optimized development solution and underpins our integrated commercial model." Subsea7 values the contract at between $150 million and $300 million, reflecting the award's significance amid ongoing geopolitical developments affecting global energy markets.

ExxonMobil remains the counterparty to the project, underscoring the alliance the company has with SLB OneSubsea and Subsea Integration Alliance to deliver integrated offshore solutions.

SECTION 1 SUBSEA 7 ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Subsea 7 Activities

Activities

Subsea 7 is a global leader in offshore energy services, specializing in the engineering, construction, and maintenance of subsea infrastructure for the oil and gas industry. Headquartered in London, the company delivers comprehensive solutions, including subsea umbilicals, risers, flowlines, and field development projects, supporting clients in deepwater exploration and production worldwide. Its core activities encompass project management, installation, and life-of-field services, leveraging advanced technologies to enhance operational efficiency and safety. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly offshore wind farm installations, driven by the global shift toward sustainable energy sources. This expansion has seen Subsea 7 diversify its portfolio, with increasing involvement in floating wind projects and subsea power transmission, positioning the company for sustained growth amid evolving market demands. With a strong presence in key regions like the North Sea, Gulf ofMexico, and Brazil, Subsea 7 continues to innovate, fostering long-term partnerships and contributing to the energy transition. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 14th largest Energy company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JH6-9SW3-SH32-B42X-00000-00Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) Sets Out Terafab Plan With Intel to Build Two Chip Factories in Austin2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisTesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) Sets Out Terafab Plan With Intel to Build Two Chip Factories in Austin

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

04 May 2026 11:46 EDT

Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) has outlined the Terafab project, a plan to build its own semiconductor foundry to support internal chip needs for its robots, electric vehicles and SpaceX AI data centers. On 4th May 2026, the company said Intel Corporation (NASDAQ: INTC) will assist with a capital investment exceeding $25 billion to construct two advanced chip factories in Austin, Texas. The Terafab initiative aims to reduce dependence on external suppliers such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (NYSE: TSM) and strengthen vertical integration for Tesla and SpaceX operations. Analysts note the project carries considerable risk given rapid advances in semiconductor technology and the scale of the investment, but potential rewards could come from improved efficiency and more predictable supply chains if timelines are met

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JGJ-JTH3-RSH0-R11D-00000-00Eni SpA (NYSE:E (ADR)) Invests $70 Million in Nouveau Monde Graphite Inc. (TSX:NOU (ADR)) to Strengthen Graphite Supply Chain2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisEni SpA (NYSE:E (ADR)) Invests $70 Million in Nouveau Monde Graphite Inc. (TSX:NOU (ADR)) to Strengthen Graphite Supply Chain

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

01 May 2026 12:31 EDT

Eni SpA (NYSE:E (ADR)) Invests $70 Million in Nouveau Monde Graphite Inc. (TSX:NOU (ADR)) to Strengthen Graphite Supply Chain, as part of a broader $297 million fundraising backed by the Canada Growth Fund, Investissement Quebec and public funding, on 1 May 2026. Upon closing, Eni will hold about 11.5% of Nouveau Monde Graphite's equity and secure a seat on its board to enable a closer strategic partnership.

Eni will secure exclusive supply agreements for graphite and active anode material, essential inputs for its Brindisi-based gigafactory and stationary lithium-ion battery program. Nouveau Monde Graphite will use the funds to advance the Matawinie mine, noted for high-quality natural graphite, and to process material at its Becancour Battery Material Plant in Quebec, strengthening the battery-material supply chain.

The arrangement comes with a favorable market backdrop as West Texas Intermediate crude trades above $100 per barrel. Eni holds a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), while Nouveau Monde Graphite ranks #3 (Hold).

SECTION 1 ENI S.P.A. ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Eni S.p.A. Activities

Activities

Eni SpA, an Italian multinational energy company, is a leading integrated player in the global energy sector. Its core business activities encompass the exploration, production, and marketing of oil and natural gas, as well as refining, chemicals, and power generation. The company operates across the entire energy value chain, including renewable energy projects and infrastructure development. In recent years, Eni's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as solar and wind power, which are expanding rapidly to align with global decarbonization efforts. Additionally, the natural gas business is experiencing significant growth due to increasing demand for lower-emission fuels as a bridge to a sustainable future. Eni continues to invest in innovation, including bio-fuels and carbon capture technologies, to enhance operational efficiency and support the energy transition while maintaining a strong focus on environmental stewardship. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 4th largest Oil & gas company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: Tesla Partners with Houston City College to Strengthen Workforce Training

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-WT63-RSWV-80PJ-00000-00Tesla Partners with Houston City College to Strengthen Workforce Training2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisTesla Partners with Houston City College to Strengthen Workforce Training

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

13 May 2026 11:17 EDT

Houston City College has reinforced its commitment to workforce education by signing a partnership agreement with Tesla. The collaboration, formalized on the 12th of May, 2026, is set to train local workers for careers in advanced manufacturing and has already produced its first cohort of graduates from HCC's Stafford campus.

The newly signed Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) establishes a framework for both organizations to jointly develop curriculum, share resources, and create direct training pathways for participants. The initial 12 graduates completed HCC's Industrial Maintenance and Advanced Manufacturing program, gaining proficiency in essential skills such as maintenance, troubleshooting, and adherence to industry safety standards. All graduates are expected to begin their careers at Tesla's Megafactory Texas located in Brookshire, where the company manufactures its Megapack 3 energy storage units.

During the signing ceremony, HCC Chancellor Margaret Ford Fisher highlighted the importance of creating strong industry connections to offer meaningful career opportunities for students and the community. "By partnering with Tesla, we are taking a leading role in meeting the demands of today's advanced manufacturing industry for our region," Fisher stated, emphasizing the collaborative nature of the program.

Tesla's Director of Megafactory Operations, Javier Corral Astorga, echoed this sentiment, stating, "We are proud to partner with Houston City College to help prepare the next generation of manufacturing professionals." Astorga noted the program's potential to equip individuals with the technical skills and real-world experience necessary to thrive in the rapidly growing manufacturing sector.

The MOU not only aims to train individuals but also sets a goal to expand enrollment to 250 students by the end of the year. HCC Board of Trustees Chair Eva Loredo described the partnership as a forward-thinking approach to workforce education, emphasizing the alignment of classroom instruction with hands-on industry experience. This collaboration marks a significant step in supporting the economic growth of the region while addressing the skills gap in advanced manufacturing.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: Ron Baron Signals Continued Investment in Tesla and SpaceX

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-WT63-RSWV-80PM-00000-00Ron Baron Signals Continued Investment in Tesla and SpaceX2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisRon Baron Signals Continued Investment in Tesla and SpaceX

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

13 May 2026 11:17 EDT

In a recent appearance on CNBC's Squawk Box, legendary investor Ron Baron reaffirmed his commitment to investing in Tesla, Inc. and SpaceX, noting that his firm, Baron Capital, will continue to acquire shares in both companies. The interview took place on 12th May 2026, where Baron praised CEO Elon Musk's visionary leadership and innovative approach.

Baron, who manages approximately $55 billion in assets, detailed how his investments in SpaceX have surged from $1.7 billion in 2017 to over $15 billion, making it Baron Capital's largest holding. Tesla follows closely, valued at around $5 billion in the portfolio. Together, these investments highlight Musk's ability to reshape industries through groundbreaking technology, with Baron describing them as generational opportunities.

A significant focus during the discussion was SpaceX's anticipated initial public offering (IPO), expected in mid-2026. Baron disclosed plans to purchase an additional $1 billion in shares at the IPO. He expressed strong belief in SpaceX's potential, predicting that it could become the largest company globally, driven by projects like Starlink and reusable rockets.

Baron also expressed optimism about Tesla, forecasting that its stock could reach between $2,000 and $2,500 per share within a decade, which would imply a market capitalization of approximately $8.3 trillion. He emphasized Tesla's transition beyond electric vehicles into advanced technologies such as AI and robotics, viewing these developments as catalysts for significant revenue growth.

Baron's unwavering support for Musk and his companies aligns with a long-term investment philosophy, as he has stated he does not plan to sell any shares of Tesla or SpaceX during his lifetime. This approach underscores the belief that the best days for both companies are still ahead, urging investors to remain patient through market fluctuations.

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

May 3rd, 2026 ( DailyPolitical - Delivered by Newstex )

Montauk Renewables ( NASDAQ:MNTK - Get Free Report) is anticipated to post its Q1 2026 results after the market closes on Wednesday, May 6th. Analysts expect Montauk Renewables to post earnings of ($0.02) per share and revenue of $42.8420 million for the quarter. Individuals may review the information on the company's upcoming Q1 2026 earning report for the latest details on the call scheduled for Thursday, May 7, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET.

Montauk Renewables ( NASDAQ:MNTK - Get Free Report) last released its earnings results on Wednesday, March 11th. The company reported $0.02 earnings per share for the quarter, missing the consensus estimate of $0.06 by ($0.04). The business had revenue of $43.39 million for the quarter, compared to analysts' expectations of $44.39 million. Montauk Renewables had a net margin of 0.15% and a return on equity of 0.10%. On average, analysts expect Montauk Renewables to post $0 EPS for the current fiscal year and $0 EPS for the next fiscal year.

Montauk Renewables Stock Down 1.8%

Shares of NASDAQMNTK traded down $0.03 during trading hours on Monday, hitting $1.38. The stock had a trading volume of 172,159 shares, compared to its average volume of 401,227. The company has a market capitalization of $196.96 million, a P/E ratio of 68.78 and a beta of 0.47. The firm's 50-day moving average price is $1.34 and its two-hundred day moving average price is $1.62. Montauk Renewables has a 52 week low of $1.07 and a 52 week high of $2.78. The company has a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.48, a quick ratio of 1.11 and a current ratio of 1.11.

Institutional Investors Weigh In On Montauk Renewables

Institutional investors and hedge funds have recently added to or reduced their stakes in the stock. BNP Paribas Financial Markets lifted its holdings in Montauk Renewables by 92.3% in the 3rd quarter. BNP Paribas Financial Markets now owns 13,175 shares of the company's stock worth $26,000 after purchasing an additional 6,323 shares during the last quarter. Virtu Financial LLC acquired a new position in shares of Montauk Renewables during the third quarter worth approximately $28,000. Two Sigma Investments LP acquired a new position in shares of Montauk Renewables during the third quarter worth approximately $34,000. Corient Private Wealth LLC acquired a new position in shares of Montauk Renewables during the second quarter worth approximately $36,000. Finally, HRT Financial LP acquired a new position in shares of Montauk Renewables during the fourth quarter worth approximately $44,000. 16.35% of the stock is currently owned by institutional investors and hedge funds.

Wall Street Analysts Forecast Growth

Several equities research analysts recently commented on MNTK shares. Scotiabank lowered their price target on Montauk Renewables from $4.00 to $2.00 and set a "sector perform" rating for the company in a research note on Wednesday, April 22nd. UBS Group lowered their price target on Montauk Renewables from $2.85 to $1.60 and set a "neutral" rating for the company in a research note on Friday, April 24th. Wall Street Zen raised Montauk Renewables from a "sell" rating to a "hold" rating in a research note on Saturday, March 7th. Zacks Research raised Montauk Renewables from a "hold" rating to a "strong-buy" rating in a research note on Wednesday, April 15th. Finally, Weiss Ratings reissued a "sell (d)" rating on shares of Montauk Renewables in a research note on Tuesday, April 21st. One equities research analyst has rated the stock with a Strong Buy rating, three have given a Hold rating and one has issued a Sell rating to the company. Based on data from MarketBeat.com, the company has a consensus rating of "Hold" and an average target price of $1.80.

View Our Latest Report on Montauk Renewables

About Montauk Renewables

( Get Free Report)

Montauk Renewables Holdings, Inc is a renewable energy company headquartered in Irving, Texas, specializing in the capture and conversion of landfill gas into clean energy products. The company's core operations focus on the design, development and operation of landfill gas collection systems that extract methane and other biogases generated by municipal solid waste. Montauk processes this gas into renewable natural gas (RNG) suitable for pipeline injection and also generates electricity for sale to utilities and commercial consumers.

Through its subsidiaries, Montauk provides a suite of environmental and wastemanagement services across the United States and Canada.

value.Title: Tesla, Inc. ( NASDAQ : TSLA ) Seeks European FSD Approval as Stock Rally Persists
value.Overview: Global Round Up - Bullish and Bearish Signals, 666words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJN-SVX3-S0MR-H3X1-00000-00Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Seeks European FSD Approval as Stock Rally Persists2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisTesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Seeks European FSD Approval as Stock Rally Persists

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

11 May 2026 10:05 EDT

Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) is navigating a complex regulatory landscape in Europe as it seeks approval for its Full Self-Driving software. Discussions with Irish regulators are reportedly underway, following a stock move that saw the shares rise to $428.35 on 10th May 2026. The rally tracks gains from robust sales in China and renewed optimism surrounding artificial intelligence applications. Tesla markets FSD as a comprehensive driving solution, but critics say it falls short of true autonomy, requiring drivers to stay attentive and ready to take control when needed.

Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) sits at a market capitalization of about $1.5 trillion as it advances its software ambitions. The company has yet to prove FSD delivers sustained revenue, and investors weigh the potential upside against ongoing safety and regulatory questions. A provisional Dutch approval provides a signal of progress, but European regulators in Sweden, Finland, Denmark and Norway have voiced concerns about weather handling and the risk of misleading terminology; Tesla has not publicly addressed these criticisms.

In the United States, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration confirmed that the 2026 Model Y was the first to pass updated tests for advanced driver-assistance systems, while the agency continues to study FSD under low visibility conditions. In China, Tesla sold 79,478 vehicles in April, up 36 percent year over year but down from March and remaining behind BYD in the competitive landscape. CFO Vaibhav Taneja has signaled that Tesla is entering a significant capital-investment phase that could result in negative free cash flow for 2026, underscoring the importance of regulatory progress in Europe to sustain profits and stock momentum.

value.Title: Montauk Renewables, Inc. ( NASDAQ : MNTK ) Receives Average Recommendation of 'Hold' from Analysts

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JG9-GWR3-S1Y5-C3W5-00000-00Montauk Renewables, Inc. (NASDAQ:MNTK) Receives Average Recommendation of 'Hold' from Analysts2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisMontauk Renewables, Inc. (NASDAQ:MNTK) Receives Average Recommendation of 'Hold' from Analysts

April 30th, 2026 ( DailyPolitical - Delivered by Newstex )

Shares of Montauk Renewables, Inc. ( NASDAQ:MNTK - Get Free Report) have earned a consensus rating of "Hold" from the five research firms that are currently covering the stock, Marketbeat Ratings reports. One equities research analyst has rated the stock with a sell recommendation, three have issued a hold recommendation and one has assigned a strong buy recommendation to the company. The average 1-year price objective among brokers that have issued a report on the stock in the last year is $1.80.

Several equities research analysts have recently commented on the stock. UBS Group reduced their target price on shares of Montauk Renewables from $2.85 to $1.60 and set a "neutral" rating on the stock in a research note on Friday, April 24th. Weiss Ratings reaffirmed a "sell (d)" rating on shares of Montauk Renewables in a report on Tuesday, April 21st. Zacks Research raised Montauk Renewables from a "hold" rating to a "strong-buy" rating in a report on Wednesday, April 15th. Wall Street Zen raised Montauk Renewables from a "sell" rating to a "hold" rating in a report on Saturday, March 7th. Finally, Scotiabank cut their price target on Montauk Renewables from $4.00 to $2.00 and set a "sector perform" rating for the company in a report on Wednesday, April 22nd.

Get Our Latest Stock Report on MNTK

Institutional Inflows and Outflows

A number of institutional investors have recently made changes to their positions in MNTK. AQR Capital Management LLC bought a new position in shares of Montauk Renewables in the first quarter valued at about $62,000. Jane Street Group LLC raised its holdings in shares of Montauk Renewables by 303.4% in the first quarter. Jane Street Group LLC now owns 100,834 shares of the company's stock valued at $213,000 after buying an additional 75,836 shares during the last quarter. Strs Ohio bought a new position in shares of Montauk Renewables in the first quarter valued at about $84,000. JPMorgan Chase & Co. raised its holdings in shares of Montauk Renewables by 25.0% in the second quarter. JPMorgan Chase & Co. now owns 64,772 shares of the company's stock valued at $144,000 after buying an additional 12,974 shares during the last quarter. Finally, Rhumbline Advisers raised its holdings in shares of Montauk Renewables by 12.6% in the second quarter. Rhumbline Advisers now owns 105,881 shares of the company's stock valued at $235,000 after buying an additional 11,820 shares during the last quarter. 16.35% of the stock is owned by institutional investors.

Montauk Renewables Stock Up 1.4%

Shares of NASDAQ:MNTK opened at $1.46 on Friday. Montauk Renewables has a 52 week low of $1.07 and a 52 week high of $2.78. The company has a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.48, a quick ratio of 1.11 and a current ratio of 1.11. The company's fifty day moving average is $1.34 and its 200-day moving average is $1.62. The company has a market capitalization of $209.13 million, a PE ratio of 73.04 and a beta of 0.18.

Montauk Renewables ( NASDAQ:MNTK - Get Free Report) last issued its earnings results on Wednesday, March 11th. The company reported $0.02 EPS for the quarter, missing the consensus estimate of $0.06 by ($0.04). Montauk Renewables had a net margin of 0.15% and a return on equity of 0.10%. The firm had revenue of $43.39 million during the quarter, compared to analyst estimates of $44.39 million. Equities research analysts predict that Montauk Renewables will post 0.08 earnings per share for the current fiscal year.

Montauk Renewables Company Profile

( Get Free Report)

Montauk Renewables Holdings, Inc is a renewable energy company headquartered in Irving, Texas, specializing in the capture and conversion of landfill gas into clean energy products. The company's core operations focus on the design, development and operation of landfill gas collection systems that extract methane and other biogases generated by municipal solid waste. Montauk processes this gas into renewable natural gas (RNG) suitable for pipeline injection and also generates electricity for sale to utilities and commercial consumers.

Through its subsidiaries, Montauk provides a suite of environmental and wastemanagement services across the United States and Canada.

value.Title: Tesla's Robotaxi Expansion and Surge in French Sales Highlight a Busy Week

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJF-GFY3-RW2W-R35W-00000-00Tesla's Robotaxi Expansion and Surge in French Sales Highlight a Busy Week2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisTesla's Robotaxi Expansion and Surge in French Sales Highlight a Busy Week

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

11 May 2026 07:15 EDT

Tesla, Inc. continues to make headlines with significant developments in its operations and sales strategies. On 11th May 2026, the electric vehicle manufacturer ramped up its Robotaxi operations in Texas, deploying over 20 unsupervised Robotaxis in Austin, along with three each in Houston and Dallas. Despite this progress, Tesla is still trailing behind competitors like Waymo, which continues to lead in the autonomous vehicle sector.

The week also marked a notable surge in Tesla's sales across Europe, particularly in France and Sweden. French sales skyrocketed by 112% in April, while Sweden experienced a remarkable growth of 111%. However, not all regions reported positive trends; Norway saw a significant decline in sales, dropping by 61%. This mixed performance highlights the varying demand for electric vehicles in different European markets.

In a separate development, Tesla announced a recall affecting over 218,000 vehicles in the United States. The recall, which encompasses the Model S, 3, X, and Y, was initiated due to a delay in the rearview camera image when the vehicle is placed in reverse. To address this issue, Tesla has rolled out a software update, aiming to mitigate any potential safety concerns.

Elon Musk's ambitious vision for Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology also drew attention this week, as EU regulators raised concerns regarding its performance in icy conditions. Musk's $1 trillion pay package has come under scrutiny, especially given the regulatory focus on the safety features of the FSD system. Investors are keenly observing these developments as they could impact Tesla's market position and investor confidence.

Finally, Tesla secured a significant order for its Semi truck, with WattEV placing an order for over 370 units. This deal represents the largest electric vehicle truck order in California's history, further solidifying Tesla's position in the commercial electric vehicle market. As the company continues to innovate and expand its offerings, stakeholders remain eager to see how these developments will influence Tesla's growth trajectory.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: Tesla and SpaceX Set to Build Advanced Chip Plants in Austin

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-JYG3-RT56-72MJ-00000-00Tesla and SpaceX Set to Build Advanced Chip Plants in Austin2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisTesla and SpaceX Set to Build Advanced Chip Plants in Austin

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

08 May 2026 07:16 EDT

Elon Musk announced that Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) will join forces with SpaceX to establish two advanced semiconductor manufacturing plants in Austin, Texas. The announcement, made on 6th May 2026, outlines ambitious plans for one facility dedicated to producing chips for vehicles and humanoid robots, while the other will focus on supplying artificial intelligence data centers operating in space.

The proposed semiconductor complex, dubbed 'Terafab,' will consist of two fabrication plants, each responsible for a single chip design. Musk emphasized the pressing need for these facilities, stating that without the Terafab, Tesla would struggle to meet its chip requirements. The global semiconductor market is currently unable to keep pace with the projected demand from Tesla and SpaceX, which Musk believes could soon outstrip the world's total chip production capacity.

While Musk has hinted at the need for a dedicated chip production facility for Tesla in the past, the collaboration with SpaceX marks a significant development in the tech landscape. SpaceX, which is reportedly preparing for a potential public listing with an estimated valuation of $1.75 trillion, recently integrated with Musk's artificial intelligence venture, xAI. This partnership could potentially enhance the technological capabilities of both companies.

The Terafab facility is expected to produce around one terawatt of computing capacity annually, a figure that dwarfs the current total computing capacity generated across the United States, which is approximately half a terawatt. One of the chips will be tailored for Tesla vehicles and the company's Optimus humanoid robots, while the second will be specifically designed for AI satellites operating in space, taking into account the unique challenges of the environment.

Musk acknowledged the existing semiconductor suppliers, including major players like Samsung, TSMC, and Micron, but reiterated that the demand from Tesla could eventually exceed their combined output. As the automotive and aerospace industries increasingly rely on advanced AI technologies, the establishment of these chip plants could play a pivotal role in shaping the future of both Tesla and SpaceX.

value.Title: Tesla 's Self-Driving Software Eyes Approval in Flanders

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-DPW3-RXP3-K37C-00000-00Tesla's Self-Driving Software Eyes Approval in Flanders2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisTesla's Self-Driving Software Eyes Approval in Flanders

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 12:02 EDT

BRUSSELS, May 5 (Reuters) - Tesla's aim to roll out its supervised 'full self-driving' software in Europe received a significant boost from Belgium's Flanders region on Tuesday. The region announced it is exploring the possibility of quickly adopting the technology, following its provisional approval in the Netherlands last month.

The Dutch regulator's decision marked a milestone, making the Netherlands the first EU country to permit Tesla's software, which allows vehicles to control themselves under the condition that drivers remain attentive. Annick De Ridder, the transport minister for Flanders, stated on social media that she has requested documentation from Tesla to facilitate a potential similar approval in her region.

De Ridder emphasized the importance of fostering innovation while ensuring safety, asserting that Flanders aims to maintain its reputation as a forward-thinking region. She indicated that her team would clarify by the end of the week whether a fast-track approval process for Tesla's software is feasible.

Despite the optimism from Tesla's leadership, including CEO Elon Musk, the technology has faced skepticism from regulators in several other EU countries such as Sweden, Finland, Denmark, and Norway, regarding its safety claims. A spokesperson for Belgium's central transport ministry noted that the decision to approve the software lies with regional authorities rather than the national government.

In addition to its efforts in Flanders, Tesla has submitted a request to Belgium's primarily French-speaking region, Wallonia. The spokesperson for Brussels, which is a separate region, did not provide immediate comments on the matter.

May 12th, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

Tesla, IREN, and Coterra Energy are the three Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, refining, transporting, or supplying energy, such as oil, natural gas, coal, and renewable power. For stock market investors, this sector includes businesses like major oil producers, pipeline operators, utility companies, and clean energy firms, and their performance is often influenced by commodity prices, regulation, and global demand for energy. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla (TSLA)

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN (IREN)

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

May 11th, 2026 ( Transcript Daily - Delivered by Newstex )

Tesla, IREN, and Coterra Energy are the three Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, refining, transporting, or supplying energy, such as oil, natural gas, coal, and renewable power. For stock market investors, this sector includes businesses like major oil producers, pipeline operators, utility companies, and clean energy firms, and their performance is often influenced by commodity prices, regulation, and global demand for energy. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla (TSLA)

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN (IREN)

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Coterra Energy (CTRA)

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

value.Title: Stocks to Watch today: Adani Enterprises , Vodafone Idea, Tata Tech, D-Mart

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGW-YGJ3-S09H-K55C-00000-00Stocks to Watch today: Adani Enterprises, Vodafone Idea, Tata Tech, D-Mart2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisStocks to Watch today: Adani Enterprises, Vodafone Idea, Tata Tech, D-Mart

Stocks to Watch today, Monday, May 4, 2026: The GIFT Nifty futures indicated a favorable start for the benchmark Indian equity indices in the week's first trading session. At 06:53 AM, the futures were quoted at 24,258, up 160 points from the previous close.


Among global peers, South Korean stocks rose to a fresh record high, following their strongest monthly gain in April. Investors remained cautious amid ongoing tensions between Iran and the United States, along with a US initiative to reopen shipping routes in the Strait of Hormuz. The Kospi index was trading 2.81 per cent higher, while Australia's S&P/ASX 200 slipped 0.22 per cent.
US President Donald Trump announced on Truth Social that the United States would attempt to "free" ships stranded due to the Strait of Hormuz closure since the start of the Iran conflict. The initiative, called "Project Freedom," will focus on assisting civilian vessels from non-conflict countries to exit the contested waterway safely.
Oil prices declined following the announcement. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) futures for July delivery fell 0.59 per cent to $101.34 per barrel, while Brent crude futures dropped 0.27 per cent to $107.88 per barrel.
Markets in Japan and China remained closed due to public holidays.
On Friday, May 1, 2026, on Wall Street, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq settled at record high closing levels and ended with gains of 0.29 per cent and 0.89 per cent, respectively, supported by strong corporate earnings and a decline in crude oil prices. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.31 per cent. Meanwhile, here are some of the top stocks to watch during today's session: Auto stocks: Shares of Hero MotoCorp, Maruti Suzuki India, Escorts Kubota, Hyundai Motor India, Mahindra & Mahindra, Atul Auto, Tata Motors (Passenger and Commercial Vehicles), and Eicher Motors will remain in focus following April sales data.
Adani Enterprises: The company reported a consolidated net loss of ₹166.79 crore in Q4FY26, compared with a profit of ₹4,014.90 crore a year ago, as higher depreciation and other costs weighed on earnings. Total income rose to ₹33,187.11 crore from ₹27,601.64 crore.
Avenue Supermarts (D-Mart): The company posted a 19.17 per cent year-on-year rise in consolidated net profit at ₹656.42 crore in Q4FY26, up from ₹550.79 crore in the same quarter last year. Revenue increased 18.9 per cent to ₹17,683.86 crore.
Central Depository Services (India): The depository's consolidated net profit stood at ₹80 crore in Q4FY26 against ₹100 crore a year ago. Total income rose to ₹268 crore from ₹256 crore.
Kotak Mahindra Bank: The private sector lender's consolidated net profit rose 10 per cent year-on-year to ₹5,423 crore in Q4FY26. Standalone net profit increased 13 per cent to ₹4,027 crore from ₹3,552 crore, aided by lower provisions and improving asset quality.
Tata Power: Tata Power Renewable Energy Limited (TPREL) approved a new business line-Photovoltaic (PV) ingot and wafer manufacturing-with an estimated investment of ₹6,500 crore.
Vodafone Idea: The telecom operator has informed the exchanges that DoT informed that the committee finalized AGR dues at ₹64,046 crore as of December 31, 2025.
Siemens: The company has announced that it has received an internal work allocation from its group company for manufacturing bogies, traction motors, and gearboxes valued at ₹1,825 crore on March 25, 2026.
NCC: The company has secured four orders in April 2026 totaling ₹1,703.27 crore (excl. GST), including ₹929.96 crore for Buildings, ₹603.41 crore for Electrical, and ₹169.90 crore for Transportation divisions.
Bank of India: The bank's board has approved capital raising up to ₹7,500 crore via Basel-III compliant Tier-I and Tier-II bonds (₹2,500 crore and ₹5,000 crore, respectively) for FY27. Q4 results today Tata Technologies, Tata Chemicals, Bharat Heavy Electricals, Ambuja Cements, Godrej Properties, KEI Industries, Petronet LNG, Ather Energy, Exide Industries, Manappuram Finance, Aditya Birla Capital, Jindal Stainless, Wockhardt, Aarti Industries, Computer Age Management Services, Sobha, Jaiprakash Power Ventures, Jyothy Labs, IIFL Capital Services, SG MART, CSB Bank, Kothari Sugars and Chemicals, Infinity Infoway, Desco Infratech, SNL Bearings, Key Corporation, Nalin Lease Finance, Southern Magnesium and Chemicals, Thakral Services (India), Accord Synergy, Nacl Industries, Quess Corp, Antelopus Selan Energy, Indokem, Oriental Hotels, Krishival Foods, South West Pinnacle Exploration, Shiva Cement, De Nora India, Nath Bio-Genes, Shera Energy, Atvo Enterprises.

value.Title: Texas Sees Surge in Gas Projects as Data Centers Drive Shift in Power Generation

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-K8D3-RTH8-T3R6-00000-00Texas Sees Surge in Gas Projects as Data Centers Drive Shift in Power Generation2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisTexas Sees Surge in Gas Projects as Data Centers Drive Shift in Power GenerationBrandon Mulder, The Texas Tribune

A decade ago, wind power was surging in popularity and attracting huge investments that made Texas a national leader in renewable energy. But today, gas generation is making a big comeback, driven by a wave of data centers flooding into the state.

For the last six months, the volume of gas generation in the Texas grid's interconnection queue - the yearslong waiting list for electric generators wanting to connect to the grid - has surpassed wind. It's the first time since January 2016 that gas has overtaken wind in the queue, a shift that reflects the policy and economic headwinds facing the wind industry and data centers favoring gas power as they seek to cash in on the artificial intelligence boom.

"The data center explosion and their desire for 24/7 power probably excited a lot of gas developers, and that gas queue got bigger," said University of Texas professor of energy regulation David Spence.

Like every power grid operator in the U.S., the Electric Reliability Council of Texas, or ERCOT, uses the interconnection queue to manage and plan for new power generation coming online. But not all projects in the queue ultimately reach completion. According to the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, or LBNL, only 22% of projects in ERCOT's queue actually get built, which is still the highest percentage of any grid in the nation.

Still, the queue gives an early indication of how the grid is projected to evolve in the future. Solar and battery projects dominate, accounting for 75% of the 458,000 megawatts in the queue, with gas and wind projects making up the rest.

But over the last three years, the volume of gas projects in the queue has jumped by more than 400%, from 12,500 megawatts in March 2023 to nearly 64,000 megawatts as of last month, according to ERCOT data. Meanwhile, wind projects have grown at a much slower clip, increasing by 87% from 25,700 to 48,000 megawatts over the same period.

The gas queue is getting a boost from the Texas Energy Fund, a program passed by the Legislature in 2023 that provides low-interest loans to developers of so-called dispatchable power that can easily be ramped up or down according to grid demand, such as gas-fired power plants.

About 9,000 megawatts worth of projects in the queue are getting loans from the TEF, a large portion of which are "near-term gas projects we expect to come online," an ERCOT spokesperson said.

But the strongest driving force behind the gas ramp-up is the long list of companies looking to build data centers in Texas.

ERCOT data shows around 360,000 megawatts of power demand tied to prospective data center projects - a volume that would by itself more than quadruple the grid's record peak demand of 85,500 megawatts set in August 2023.

If anything close to that number actually gets built, ERCOT CEO Pablo Vegas told a Senate committee earlier this month, "then we're going to clearly need a lot more of [gas] generation in order to have a balanced and reliable grid."

ERCOT's gas embrace

Vegas sees the growth of gas projects on the queue as a welcome trend. For too long, he said, ERCOT's energy market hasn't been attracting dispatchable energy generation like gas plants.

It's a symptom of how the market was designed 25 years ago, when the Texas Legislature passed sweeping reforms that deregulated the market. Restructuring in 1999 created a competitive wholesale and retail power market and introduced a renewable energy requirement.

That incentivized bringing cheap electricity to the grid, Vegas said, and it kick-started renewable energy development that boomed through the late 2000s and early 2010s.

But Vegas said the new market design failed to value the different strengths of power sources, and those differences matter for grid reliability.

Renewables provide cheap, clean electricity but are only intermittently available when the sun is shining and wind is blowing. Battery power - which pulls electricity from the grid during off-peak hours then sends it back to the grid during peak hours - can be dispatched when needed, but it has duration limitations.

Thermal generators like gas and coal don't have similar limits as long as the fuel is available to run them.

"We've seen this explosion of wind and solar, and now batteries, to the complete exclusion of growth in the natural gas system, because economically we're not valuing the characteristics of gas generation that is so important for long-term reliability," Vegas said. "We need to change that somewhere in the market design to recognize the reliability characteristics of the generating source.

But bringing gas-fired power onto the grid faces its own hurdles. For the last several years, the supply chain for gas turbines - the engines that power plants use to generate electricity - has seen a major bottleneck caused in large part by surging demand from data centers outstripping manufacturing capacity. According to Wood Mackenzie, an energy research and consulting firm, orders for a turbine today may take until 2031 to arrive.

However, data centers and gas power developers are finding creative ways around this problem, including repurposing turbines originally designed for aircraft or cruise ships, according to a recent report from the energy data company Cleanview.

In El Paso, for instance, the city's electric utility is planning to build a gas plant using a novel design that will tie together 813 small gas generators, which are typically used as backup power units at hospitals or manufacturing facilities, to produce 366 megawatts for a data center being built by Meta. And in Mississippi, Elon Musk's AI company xAI purchased and revived a dormant power plant last year to power a data center that helps train xAI's chatbot Grok.

"The perceived economic loss of not getting your data center up and running in a year is valued in the billions of dollars," said University of Texas grid researcher Joshua Rhodes. "That will drive people to very out-of-the-box solutions for these types of things."

Wind's slowdown

On the other side of the equation is wind's shrinking market share. Since its peak in 2018, when wind composed around 50% of the megawatts in the interconnection queue, wind has now fallen behind every other major generation source.

The headwinds facing wind today are multifold, experts say, and extend beyond the recent cuts to tax credits for renewable projects.

Wind's primary challenge stems from its earlier success. After two decades of immense growth across the state, all of the prime development locations have been taken. Parcels across West Texas and the coastal region - where winds are strong, land is accessible, and transmission lines are available to send the electricity to the grid - have mostly been developed already.

"Texas has a lot of land, but the low-hanging fruit has been picked over by all the wind development that has happened there," said Joseph Rand, an energy markets researcher at LBNL.

And because the West Texas and coastal regions have become saturated, the existing transmission lines have grown congested, forcing generators to curtail their power output - which cuts into their profits.

To solve that issue, ERCOT is advancing plans to update its transmission system again, which will include three major 765 kilovolt lines capable of carrying more electricity than any existing line in Texas.

"If I were a wind developer, I might not want to build today and face that curtailment risk for the first few years of my project's lifespan," Rand said.

Wind's challenges also stem from solar's soaring success in Texas. While technology costs for wind and solar have both fallen over the last two decades, the cost of solar energy has fallen more rapidly, from around $160 per megawatt-hour in 2010 to around $70 in 2024, LBNL data shows.

"Because the cost of building a solar farm has gone down so dramatically over the last five to six years, it just becomes a better business decision to build solar and, increasingly, solar plus [battery] storage," said Judd Messer, Texas vice president of the clean energy trade association Advanced Power Alliance.

Lastly, the wind industry faces policy uncertainty that has made it difficult for wind projects to attract financing. Trump administration policies that have slowed or paused federal approvals necessary for wind projects to reach completion have caused investors to shy away from the industry.

In Texas, which created a grid designed to avoid federal regulation by operating almost entirely within state lines, the federal government can still hamper renewables via the Federal Aviation Administration's 200-foot rule, which requires construction permits for all structures over 200 feet tall, such as onshore wind turbines.

Last summer, for instance, the U.S. Department of Transportation announced that the FAA "will thoroughly evaluate proposed wind turbines to ensure they do not pose a danger to aviation," indicating that the routine permit may become a hurdle for onshore wind.

"This kind of arbitrary executive-level uncertainty is really spooking people," said Messer.

value.Title: Endesa (OTC: ELEZY (ADR)) Secures LNG Carrier Adriano Knutsen on Long-Term Charter

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-XJ53-RSYN-929F-00000-00Endesa (OTC: ELEZY (ADR)) Secures LNG Carrier Adriano Knutsen on Long-Term Charter2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisEndesa (OTC: ELEZY (ADR)) Secures LNG Carrier Adriano Knutsen on Long-Term Charter

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 16:15 EDT

Endesa (OTC: ELEZY (ADR)) signs a long-term LNG carrier charter for the Adriano Knutsen with Knutsen OAS Shipping, effective next summer, in a move that shifts the company toward time charters for LNG imports. The transaction, disclosed on May 5, 2026, marks Endesa's first such charter arrangement after previously relying on CIF purchase contracts. In 2014, Endesa secured a 20-year deal to buy two million cubic meters of natural gas annually from Cheniere Energy's Corpus Christi project in Texas.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 ENDESA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Endesa Activities

Activities

Endesa, a leading Spanish energy company and subsidiary of Enel Group, operates primarily in the electricity sector across Spain and Portugal. Its core business activities encompass electricity generation from a diverse mix of sources, including renewables like wind and solar, as well as thermal and hydroelectric power; distribution and supply of electricity and natural gas; and customer-focused services such as energy efficiency solutions. In recent years, Endesa's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, driven by significant investments in wind and solar projects to meet rising demand for sustainable power and comply with EU decarbonization targets. Additionally, the company is expanding in electric mobility and smart grid technologies, positioning itself as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy while maintaining a strong focus on operational efficiency and customer service. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 4th largest Electrical company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: How Sempra Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-4CF3-RYDK-81B5-00000-00How Sempra Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisHow Sempra Ranks Among Companies with 2025 Investments Exceeding $1 Billion *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

During President Trump's second term, corporate investment in the United States has increased, with major companies in technology, manufacturing, and healthcare allocating billions to domestic expansion. The semiconductor industry has committed over $500 billion in private-sector funding, indicating a notable reshoring trend. This investment activity is backed by enhanced tax incentives from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, including 100% bonus depreciation and 35% manufacturing credits, and tariff policies aimed at supporting domestic production.

The reshoring trend affects multiple high-value sectors. Semiconductor manufacturing investments of over $500 billion across U.S. facilities are expected to create more than 150,000 new jobs, while pharmaceutical companies have allocated over $150 billion for U.S. manufacturing investments. Foreign direct investment was recorded at $102 billion in Q2 2025, representing a 137% increase from Q1. This comprehensive restructuring is influenced by both policy incentives and supply chain resilience priorities.

This report examines investment commitments from companies across the technology, manufacturing, healthcare, and energy sectors.

Sempra (NYSE: SRE)

- Company Profile: Sempra (NYSE: SRE) is a U.S.-headquartered energy infrastructure firm operating utilities and LNG facilities, with a global workforce of around 20,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A $3.5 billion capital injection for 2025-2026 is intended to construct the Port Arthur LNG facility in Texas, grow natural gas infrastructure, and improve renewable energy projects.

- Recipient: Sempra U.S. Energy Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Energy, Utilities

Phillips 66 (NYSE: PSX)

- Company Profile: Phillips 66 is a U.S.-based energy manufacturing and logistics firm that manages refineries and pipelines, employing around 13,000 people globally.

- Announced Investment: An allocation of $1.2 billion is planned for 2025 to enhance U.S. refineries for renewable fuels production and to grow midstream infrastructure.

- Recipient: Phillips 66 U.S. Refining and Pipeline Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Energy, Infrastructure

Plexus Corp. (NASDAQ: PLXS)

- Company Profile: Plexus Corp. is a U.S.-based electronics manufacturing services provider with a global staff of approximately 11,000 people.

- Announced Investment: A sum of $1.3 billion is scheduled for 2025 to increase advanced electronics assembly and semiconductor manufacturing.

- Recipient: Plexus U.S. Manufacturing Facilities

- Industries Impacted: Electronics, Manufacturing

PPL Corporation (NYSE: PPL)

- Company Profile: PPL Corporation is a U.S.-based utility that delivers electricity to its customers, with a global employee base of approximately 12,000.

- Announced Investment: A total of $6.8 billion is slated for 2025-2026 to grow grid capacity, update transmission infrastructure, and integrate renewable energy in its Pennsylvania and Kentucky operations.

- Recipient: PPL U.S. Electric Grid Infrastructure

- Industries Impacted: Utilities, Energy

PulteGroup Inc. (NYSE: PHM)

- Company Profile: PulteGroup Inc. is a U.S.-based homebuilder involved in creating residential communities, with a workforce of approximately 6,000 people.

- Announced Investment: An amount of $1 billion is planned for 2025 to fund the creation of housing projects located in Arizona.

- Recipient: PulteGroup U.S. Residential Developments

- Industries Impacted: Construction, Real Estate

Public Service Enterprise Group (NYSE: PEG)

- Company Profile: Public Service Enterprise Group is a U.S.-based energy firm managing utilities and power generation, employing approximately 13,000 people globally.

- Announced Investment: A sum of $3.2 billion is designated for 2025-2026 to build offshore wind infrastructure, update transmission systems, and increase clean energy generation in New Jersey.

- Recipient: Public Service Enterprise Group U.S. Energy Infrastructure

Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

value.Title: Tesla, Inc. ( NASDAQ : TSLA ) Posts Mixed Quarterly Update as AI and Robotics Pivot Gains Traction

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-9SW3-SH32-B42Y-00000-00Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Posts Mixed Quarterly Update as AI and Robotics Pivot Gains Traction2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisTesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Posts Mixed Quarterly Update as AI and Robotics Pivot Gains Traction

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

04 May 2026 11:45 EDT

Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Posts Mixed Quarterly Update on 4th May 2026, with revenue missing analyst expectations while earnings beat forecasts, underscoring a mixed near term picture. The update highlights a strategic pivot toward diversification beyond its electric vehicle core, including accelerated production of the Optimus humanoid robot and expansion of the robotaxi service. Tesla CEO Elon Musk signaled a substantial increase in capital expenditures to fund this vision, with emphasis on AI software, AI training, chip design, and battery powertrains. While Tesla continues to develop its own AI chips, it relies on Nvidia for AI training, reflecting a collaborative relationship rather than a shift away from Nvidia.

Observers say the new emphasis on robotics and AI could influence Nvidia's AI ecosystem as both firms align on broader deployment of automation technologies. Nvidia remains a leading provider of AI training platforms, and the joint evolution of Tesla's robotics and Nvidia's software stack could reshape the automotive and tech landscapes in the coming quarters.

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.
