urn:contentItem:6JHT-SDR3-S579-R4H4-00000-00Spartan Chemical Promotes Riley to Regional Manage2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisNEWSSpartan Chemical Promotes Riley to Regional Manage

Spartan Chemical Company, Inc., a recognized leader in the formulation and manufacture of sustainable cleaning and sanitation solutions for...

Spartan Chemical Company, Inc., a recognized leader in the formulation and manufacture of sustainable cleaning and sanitation solutions for the industrial and institutional market, today announced the promotion of Nelson Riley to the role of regional manag...

Spartan Chemical Company, Inc., a recognized leader in the formulation and manufacture of sustainable cleaning and sanitation solutions for the industrial and institutional market, today announced the promotion of Nelson Riley to the role of regional manager, effective, March 16, 2026. Nelson joins Spartan in the South Texas region. For the past year, Mr. Riley has held the role of sales business development for Foxguard Solutions where he was responsible for partnering with businesses to provide cutting-edge industrial computing solutions that exceeded customer expectations. Prior to his time with Foxguard Solutions, Nelson worked for Texas Solar in San Antonio, Texas, for four years. During his time with Texas Solar, he held the position of sales manager, where he was responsible for leading a team of dynamic solar consultants to educate homeowners about the benefits of renewable energy. Finally, Mr. Riley worked for Lindsey Dental in San Antonio, Texas, for three years as the practice manager. In this role, he managed daily operations, marketing, and sales of multiple dental offices. Nelson attended West Texas A&M University, in Canyon, Texas where he received his master’s degree in business administration.

MAUMEE, Ohio -- Spartan Chemical Company, a manufacturer of sustainable cleaning and sanitation solutions for the industrial and institutional market, has promoted Nelson Riley to regional manager for South Texas. For the past year, Riley has held the role of sales business development for Foxguard Solutions, where he was responsible for partnering with businesses to provide cutting-edge industrial computing solutions. Prior to his time with Foxguard Solutions, he worked for Texas Solar in San Antonio, Texas, for four years. During his time there, he held the position of sales manager, where he was responsible for leading a team of dynamic solar consultants to educate homeowners about the benefits of renewable energy. Finally, Mr. Riley worked for Lindsey Dental in San Antonio, Texas, for three years as the practice manager. In this role, he managed daily operations, marketing, and sales of multiple dental offices. Nelson attended West Texas A&M University, in Canyon, Texas where he received his master’s degree in business administration. For more, go to spartanchemical.com.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGK-2M83-RSWG-T2R8-00000-00Subsea 7 (OT:SUBCY) Reports ExxonMobil Subsea Tie-Back Contract for Redevelopment 2 Likembe Project Offshore Angola2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisSubsea 7 (OT:SUBCY) Reports ExxonMobil Subsea Tie-Back Contract for Redevelopment 2 Likembe Project Offshore Angola

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

01 May 2026 15:01 EDT

Subsea 7 (OT:SUBCY (ADR)) Subsea Integration Alliance, a partnership between Subsea 7 and SLB OneSubsea, reports ExxonMobil awarded the subsea tie-back contract for the Redevelopment 2 Likembe Project in Block 15, offshore Angola, on 1st May 2026. The contract is valued at $150 million to $300 million and covers engineering, procurement, construction, and installation for the subsea development. Project management and engineering responsibilities will be distributed across Subsea 7 offices in Paris, Luanda, Lisbon, and Sutton, while SLB OneSubsea will execute the umbilical scope from its facility in Norway with additional support from teams in Houston. David Bertin, Senior Vice President for Global Projects Centre East at Subsea 7, emphasized the company's commitment to developing local capability in Angola, aligning with Subsea 7's strategy to enhance local expertise on major offshore projects. The contract builds on a history of collaboration between the Subsea Integration Alliance and ExxonMobil, and the Redevelopment 2 Likembe Project forms a core part of Block 15 infrastructure activities, signaling continued investment in Angola's offshore resources.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 SUBSEA 7 ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Subsea 7 Activities

Activities

Subsea 7 is a global leader in offshore energy services, specializing in the engineering, construction, and maintenance of subsea infrastructure for the oil and gas industry. Headquartered in London, the company delivers comprehensive solutions, including subsea umbilicals, risers, flowlines, and field development projects, supporting clients in deepwater exploration and production worldwide. Its core activities encompass project management, installation, and life-of-field services, leveraging advanced technologies to enhance operational efficiency and safety. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly offshore wind farm installations, driven by the global shift toward sustainable energy sources. This expansion has seen Subsea 7 diversify its portfolio, with increasing involvement in floating wind projects and subsea power transmission, positioning the company for sustained growth amid evolving market demands. With a strong presence in key regions like the North Sea, Gulf ofMexico, and Brazil, Subsea 7 continues to innovate, fostering long-term partnerships and contributing to the energy transition. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 15th largest Energy company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JGT-1SK3-RY0R-X23J-00000-00Flex top gainer among Corbyn Investment Management holdings; jumps 40% in 31 days2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisFlex top gainer among Corbyn Investment Management holdings; jumps 40% in 31 days

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

Flex Ltd. (NASDAQ:FLEX), is the top gainer by % price gain (out of its top 20 largest holdings by market cap), of Corbyn Investment Management Inc.,MD (CIK:0000919538) holdings (assuming there has been no significant change in the holdings since March 31, 2026).

FLEX has soared $26.24 (or 40.1%) from $65.46 (as at March 31, 2026) to $91.70 as at today's close. Market Capitalization has surged by $9.7 billion from $24.2 billion to $33.9 billion in the past 31 days since March 31, 2026.

FLEX ACTIVITIES

Flex Ltd., a global leader in design, engineering, and manufacturing services, is headquartered in Singapore and listed on the NASDAQ. The company specializes in electronics manufacturing services (EMS), providing end-to-end solutions for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) in sectors such as automotive, healthcare, consumer electronics, and industrial technology. Its core activities include product design, supply chain optimization, and sustainable production processes, enabling clients to innovate and scale efficiently. Among its fastest-growing segments are electric vehicle components and renewable energy solutions, fueled by rising demand for eco-friendly technologies, as well as advanced medical devices driven by healthcare digitization. With a strong global presence and emphasis on operational excellence, Flex continues to adapt to market dynamics, delivering high-value, customized solutions that support technological advancements across industries. It is the NASDAQ's largest Electronics company by market capitalisation.

FLEX NEWS AND RESEARCH

09 Jul, 2025: Flex Announces Date for First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Earnings Call

AUSTIN, Texas, July 9, 2025 - Flex (NASDAQ: FLEX) will announce its first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results before the market opens on Thursday, July 24, 2025. The company will hold a conference call to discuss the results on the same day at 7:30 AM (CT) / 8:30 AM (ET).

The live webcast presentation will be available on the Flex Investor Relations (IR) website located at investors.flex.com. The webcast replay, along with supporting materials, will be available on the IR website following the conclusion of the event.

Friday May 01, 2026

Corbyn Investment Management (CIK: 0000919538) ranked by Assets Under Management (AUM) as reported is 4454 out of 8331 Funds for Q4 2025.

This corresponds to a percentile rank by AUM as reported of 47. This means there are 53% of FUNDS who have AUM greater than Corbyn Investment Management.

There has been rose in its percentile rank by AUM from 45 for Q3 2025 to 47 for Q4 2025.

Corbyn Investment Management was last ranked on February 17, 2026 soon after deadline date of Day 45 based on an analysis of completed Filings from Day 1 to Day 45 for Q4 2025 by 8331 Funds as at December 31, 2025.

The market value of the Institution's holdings is up $30 million (15%) in the 31 days from March 31 to May 01.

Corbyn Investment Management (CIK: 0000919538) filed their SEC Form 13F on Tuesday, April 28. Assets Under Management (AUM) were disclosed at $300 million as at 31 March, 2026.

AUM as reported fell after increases in two consecutive quarters. Assets Under Management as reported have rose $2.5 million or 0.8% in the past nine months.

FLEX ACTIVITIES

Flex Ltd., a global leader in design, engineering, and manufacturing services, is headquartered in Singapore and listed on the NASDAQ. The company specializes in electronics manufacturing services (EMS), providing end-to-end solutions for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) in sectors such as automotive, healthcare, consumer electronics, and industrial technology. Its core activities include product design, supply chain optimization, and sustainable production processes, enabling clients to innovate and scale efficiently. Among its fastest-growing segments are electric vehicle components and renewable energy solutions, fueled by rising demand for eco-friendly technologies, as well as advanced medical devices driven by healthcare digitization. With a strong global presence and emphasis on operational excellence, Flex continues to adapt to market dynamics, delivering high-value, customized solutions that support technological advancements across industries. It is the NASDAQ's largest Electronics company by market capitalisation.

09 Jul, 2025: Flex Announces Date for First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Earnings Call

AUSTIN, Texas, July 9, 2025 - Flex (NASDAQ: FLEX) will announce its first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results before the market opens on Thursday, July 24, 2025. The company will hold a conference call to discuss the results on the same day at 7:30 AM (CT) / 8:30 AM (ET).

The live webcast presentation will be available on the Flex Investor Relations (IR) website located at investors.flex.com. The webcast replay, along with supporting materials, will be available on the IR website following the conclusion of the event.

urn:contentItem:6JJ0-JKF3-RTJ0-H02F-00000-00Drew McKnight's Fortress Investment Group LLC: CPP Investments Announces Sale of European Non-Performing Loan Portfolio May 08, 20262026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:59ZLexisNexisDrew McKnight's Fortress Investment Group LLC: CPP Investments Announces Sale of European Non-Performing Loan Portfolio May 08, 2026

TORONTO, Canada - May 07, 2026 - Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPP Investments) today announced that it has entered into an agreement to sell its remaining interests in its European non-performing loan ("NPL") portfolio to a newly formed joint venture vehicle established between funds managed by affiliates of Arrow Global Group ("Arrow") and funds managed by affiliates of Fortress Investment Group ("Fortress").

Source: Company Website

INDEX

SECTION 1 BIOGRAPHY: DREW MCKNIGHT Co-CEO & Managing Partner

SECTION 2 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC PROFILE

SECTION 3 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 1 BIOGRAPHY: DREW MCKNIGHT Co-CEO & Managing Partner

Mr. McKnight is Co-Chief Executive Officer and a Managing Partner based in Dallas. He serves on the Board of Directors, the Leadership Committee, and is a member of several investment committees.

Prior to joining Fortress in February 2005, Mr. McKnight worked at Fir Tree Partners where he was responsible for analyzing and trading high yield and convertible bonds, bank debt, derivatives and equities for the value-based hedge fund. Prior to Fir Tree, Mr. McKnight worked at Goldman, Sachs & Co. in Leveraged Finance and the Distressed Bank Debt trading group. Mr. McKnight serves on the Board of Trustees for the American Enterprise Institute and Board of the Overseers at the Hoover Institution. He is a member of the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas's Financial Sector Advisory Council; the Board of Advisors at UVA's Center for Politics and SMU's Cox School of Business Alternative Asset Management Center; and the UT Southwestern Medical Center President's Advisory Board. In addition, Mr. McKnight is a member of the Council on Foreign Relations.

Mr. McKnight received a B.A. in Economics from the University of Virginia.

SECTION 2 FORTRESS INVESTMENT GROUP LLC PROFILE

2.1 ACTIVITIES

Fortress Investment Group LLC, founded in 1998, is a global investment firm headquartered in New York City. Employing over 900 people, it manages $48 billion in assets across private equity, credit, and real estate. Acquired by SoftBank in 2017, Fortress operates independently, focusing on distressed assets and infrastructure. In 2024, it invested $2 billion in renewable energy projects. Its culture emphasizes risk management, innovation, and agility, with training in asset valuation and fintech. Competing with Apollo Global, Fortress stands out for its credit expertise and opportunistic investments. Recent initiatives include blockchain-based real estate funds and ESG-focused portfolios, aligning with investment trends. Fortress diversified strategy ensures resilience in volatile markets.

value.Title: Hall-Effect Current Sensor Market Size To Worth USD 10.88 Billion By 2035 Research By SNS Insider

urn:contentItem:6JHT-Y1M3-RS1W-T472-00000-00Hall-Effect Current Sensor Market Size To Worth USD 10.88 Billion By 2035 Research By SNS Insider2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisHall-Effect Current Sensor Market Size To Worth USD 10.88 Billion By 2035 Research By SNS Insider

The United States Hall-Effect Current Sensors market was estimated to worth USD 0.62 billion in 2025 and is projected to increase at a CAGR of 12.12% from 2026 to 2035, reaching USD 1.92 billion. The growing use of electric vehicles, industrial automation, and smart consumer electronics, all of which require accurate location, speed, and current sensing, are driving the expansion of the hall-effect current sensor market.Austin, May 08, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Hall-Effect Current Sensor Market Size & Growth Insights:


According to the SNS Insider, “ The Hall-Effect Current Sensor Market was valued at USD 3.44 billion in 2025 and is expected to reach USD 10.88 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 12.40% over 2026-2035.”
Advanced Integration and Precision to Boost Market Growth Globally
The growing need for highly accurate, compact, and integrated sensing solutions in the automotive, industrial, and energy sectors is driving the Hall-Effect Current Sensor Market. Better sensor designs enable precise measurement of current, location, and speed for system simplification and miniaturization. Concerning the passive part to increase dependability and performance, the passive component has quick reaction times, broad operating temperature ranges, low power consumption, and global safety-compliant characteristics. These elements continue to propel market expansion and enable further usage of robots, electric vehicles, industrial automation, and renewable energy applications.

Purchase Single User PDF of Hall-Effect Current Sensor Market Report (20% Discount) @
Key Segmentation Analysis:
By Type
In 2025, the Linear Hall effect sensors segment accounted for approximately 45% share due to continuous and accurate sensing of current, position, and speed. The Bipolar Hall Effect sensors segment is expected to experience the fastest growth with a CAGR of 15.00% attributed to an increased demand for reliable magnetic switching applications, position detection, and speed sensing in automotive, industrial, and consumer electronics processes.
By Material
In 2025, the Indium Antimonide (InSb) segment accounted for approximately 40% market share driven by its high electron mobility and excellent sensitivity for accurate current, position, and speed sensing. The Indium Arsenide (InAs) segment is expected to experience the fastest growth with a CAGR of 16.64% due to its exceptional electron mobility, high sensitivity, and efficient low-voltage operation.
By Application
In 2025, the Position sensing segment accounted for approximately 40% share of the market due to increasing demand for accurate and reliable position sensing in automotive, industrial automation and consumer electronics applications. The Current sensing segment is expected to experience the fastest growth, expanding at a CAGR of 14.32% owing to the rising need of clear and efficient current measurement feature in electric vehicles globally.
By End Use
In 2025, the Automotive segment accounted for approximately 35% share due to the increasing demand for advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), electric vehicles, and motor control applications. The Industrial segment is expected to experience the fastest growth at a CAGR of 16.39% owing to the increasing need for automation, robotics, and smart manufacturing systems globally.
Regional Insights:
Due to increasing levels of industrialization, increased automobile production, and expanding consumer electronics and renewable energy system adoption, Asia-Pacific dominated the Hall-Effect Current Sensor Market in 2025 and held a 44% revenue share.
With a predicted CAGR of 14.16%, North America is anticipated to have the fastest growth in the Hall-Effect Current Sensor Market between 2026 and 2035. This growth is fueled by the growing use of electric vehicles, advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), and industrial automation technologies.

Recent Developments:

2024: AKM began mass production of its smallest, thinnest Hall element (HQ0A11), supporting precise position detection in compact devices such as smartphone cameras and robotics.

2023: Allegro acquired Crocus Technology, enhancing its magnetic sensing IP portfolio, particularly Hall sensor technology for automotive and industrial applications.

value.Title: Weekly renewables M&A round-up (May 4 - 8)

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHW-6WN3-RSDG-X4K7-00000-00Weekly renewables M&A round-up (May 4 - 8)2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisWeekly renewables M&A round-up (May 4 - 8)SeeNews

Renewables Now covered M&A stories about Scatec, Octopus, JinkoSolar and others in the past week. Below is the full list:

-- German battery maker CMBlu raises EUR 50m, reaches unicorn status

German battery maker CMBlu Energy has reached a valuation of over EUR 1 billion in a series C round, securing financing to back the deployment of a non-lithium, long-duration energy storage technology.

-- IFM's Mobius acquires Air Liquide's biogas assets in US, Europe

Houston, Texas-headquartered renewable natural gas (RNG) firm Mobius Renewables has finalised the acquisition of biogas production assets from Air Liquide across the US, France, Norway and Sweden.

-- Repsol selling 49% of 706-MW of Spanish wind, solar to Masdar - report

Spanish multi-energy group Repsol SA has agreed to sell a 49% stake in a 706-MW wind and solar farm portfolio in Spain to Abu Dhabi-based renewables company Masdar, Spanish business daily Cinco Dias reported, citing unnamed sources close to the deal.

-- Brussels clears EDF, Eurus JV for 120-MW BESS project in Poland

A special-purpose vehicle established specifically for the development, construction, and operation of a 120-MW battery energy storage system (BESS) project located in Kobiernice, Poland, will be run as a joint venture (JV) between EDF Renewables Polska and Eurus Energy Europe BV, after the European Commission said it had approved the deal for joint control of the entity.

-- German solar manufacturer Soluxtec files for insolvency

German solar module manufacturer Soluxtec GmbH filed an application for insolvency proceedings with the Bitburg district court.

-- Aspen Power acquires 6.44-MW New Jersey rooftop solar portfolio

US distributed generation firm Aspen Power said it has acquired two operating rooftop solar systems totalling 6.44 MWdc in New Jersey from real estate developer The Avidan Group.

-- Second-life battery firm Moment Energy raises over USD 40m in Series B

Canada-based EV battery repurposing firm Moment Energy said it had raised more than USD 40 million in a Series B funding round to expand manufacturing and deploy second-life energy storage systems across North America.

-- Fervo buying Baywa re's solar EPC unit in Italy

Italian technical services and energy management firm Fervo said it has acquired BayWa r.e. Power Solutions Srl, the Italy-based solar installation company of German renewables developer BayWa r.e. AG.

-- E energija sheds Lithuanian renewable project duo to Energix

Lithuanian renewables developer E energija group has offloaded two shovel-ready renewable energy projects in Lithuania to a unit of Israeli power producer Energix Renewable Energies Ltd.

-- National Bank of Egypt buys into Scatec's Obelisk project

National Bank of Egypt (NBE), the largest commercial bank in Egypt, has acquired a 20% economic interest in the gigawatt-scale Obelisk solar-plus-storage project in Nagaa Hammadi.

-- KGAL sells 75-MW Polish onshore wind portfolio to Octopus

Independent investment and asset manager KGAL GmbH & Co KG said it has sold a 75-MW portfolio of onshore wind parks in Poland to Octopus Energy Generation, a unit of Britain's Octopus Energy Group.

-- Octopus to buy European wind portfolio in EUR-584m deal

Octopus Energy Generation, a unit of Britain's Octopus Energy Group, has spent EUR 584 million on buying a 321-MW portfolio of onshore wind assets across France, Germany and Poland as part of its European expansion.

-- Morrow Batteries pushed into bankruptcy as financing efforts fail

Norwegian industrial battery technology company Morrow Batteries ASA has decided to file for bankruptcy proceedings after failing to secure fresh funding and a new industrial investor in time.

-- Vopak to buy majority stake in Dutch battery storage developer GES

Dutch tank storage company Royal Vopak said it has agreed in principle to acquire a majority stake in compatriot battery storage developer Green Energy Storage (GES), subject to a final investment decision (FID).

-- Encavis grows German wind fleet with 80-MW purchase

German independent power producer Encavis AG has acquired a roughly 80-MW onshore wind farm in the federal state of Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania, further expanding its wind fleet at home.

-- Verdian snaps up 117-MWp agrivoltaic portfolio in Italy

Dutch-based independent power producer Verdian, a portfolio company of Nuveen Infrastructure, unveiled the acquisition of a portfolio of three agrivoltaic projects in Sicily with a combined capacity of 117 MWp.

-- Elements Green acquires 300-MW Newarthill BESS project in Scotland

London-based renewables developer Elements Green has acquired the 300-MW Newarthill battery energy storage system (BESS) project in Scotland from developer Geocore, expanding its UK energy storage pipeline.

-- Ørsted buys 150-MW Salzburg BESS project in Michigan

Danish energy company Ørsted A/S has acquired the 150-MW Salzburg battery energy storage system (BESS) project in the US state ofMichigan from developer ESA Solar Energy LLC, with market intelligence firm Ascend Analytics acting as exclusive adviser on the transaction.

-- Belgian co-ops join 100-MW battery project in Ruien

A Belgian consortium comprising green power producer Aspiravi and energy cooperatives Ecopower and BeauVent, alongside Nippon Koei Energy Europe (NKEE), has started building a 100-MW/250-MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) complex in Ruien, western Belgium.

-- Nuvve's Japan unit raises funds in JKISS transaction for expansion, IPO

Nuvve Japan K.K. (NVJ), a unit of US-based energy storage aggregator Nuvve Holding Corp, said it had raised JPY 200 million through a JKISS-style convertible equity financing round to support business expansion in Japan.

-- Edisun Power plans CHF 440m deal with Smartenergy

Swiss solar power producer Edisun Power Europe AG plans a merger with its strategic partner, private investment firm Smartenergy Group AG, with the deal requiring a capital increase of up to CHF 440 million.

-- Sosteneo to buy Italian biomethane project portfolio

Asset manager Sosteneo SGR SpA has agreed to buy an Italian agricultural biomethane portfolio, which includes four fully permitted projects in Lombardy.

-- Acciona Energia divesting 361-MW wind farm portfolio in Spain - report

Spanish renewables group Acciona Energia has hired BNP Paribas to advise on the sale of a wind farm portfolio of about 361 MW in Spain, business newspaper Expansion reported, citing unnamed market sources.

-- Microporous gets funds for 65-GWh Virginia battery separators factory

US battery separator maker Microporous, which is a portfolio company of Trent Capital Partners, has received final financing for a new manufacturing facility in Danville, Virginia, and plans to begin construction as soon as May.

-- Mubadala invests in US renewable software firm Power Factors

Abu Dhabi's state-owned holding company Mubadala Investment Company has bought a minority stake in Power Factors LLC, a US provider of renewable energy operations and asset performance management software.

-- JUWI sells 25-MW wind project in Germany to Diehl Group unit

German renewables developer JUWI GmbH said it has sold the 25-MW Gundersweiler 2 wind farm project in southwestern Germany to industrial group Diehl.

-- Wave energy firm Panthalassa nets USD 140m from Peter Thiel, others

US renewable energy and ocean technology company Panthalassa has closed a USD-140-million funding round, led by entrepreneur and venture capitalist Peter Thiel, to scale its technology for wave-powered offshore AI computing systems with a pilot manufacturing site, among other initiatives.

-- JinkoSolar to cede control of GW-scale US manufacturing ops to PE firm

Private equity firm FH Capital has inked a deal to acquire, for an undisclosed amount, a 75.1% majority stake in Chinese solar manufacturer JinkoSolar's US manufacturing subsidiary, committing to expanding domestic solar and battery storage production.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-KFH3-RSGT-W2MM-00000-00Wednesday's Preview: Can Sempra (NYSE:SRE), continue its recent gains?2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisWednesday's Preview: Can Sempra (NYSE:SRE), continue its recent gains?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN DIEGO, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Sempra has shown some strength in recent days. Sempra (NYSE:SRE), the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, increased 0.6% during its previous trading session to close at $93.40.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 341 words

Sun May 10, 2026: Sempra Shares Climb Following $10 Billion Infrastructure Stake Sale

Sempra's stock price surged nearly 5% on the New York Stock Exchange following the announcement of a significant divestiture. On 10th May 2026, the San Diego-based utility holding company revealed it would sell a 45% interest in its infrastructure business to private equity firm KKR for $10 billion. This strategic move is aimed at simplifying Sempra's business model and enhancing its financial position.

The infrastructure unit being sold includes operations related to liquefied natural gas terminals, gas pipelines, and renewable energy facilities such as solar and wind generation sites. Jeffrey W. Martin, Sempra's chairman and CEO, emphasized that the transaction is designed to streamline operations and fund robust utility growth in key markets like Texas and California. He stated that this sale underscores Sempra's commitment to a long-term partnership with KKR, aligning their vision to strengthen America's global leadership in LNG exports.

Upon completion of the deal, a KKR-led consortium will hold a 65% equity stake in Sempra Infrastructure Partners, while Sempra will maintain a 25% interest and the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority will acquire the remaining 10%. Sempra indicated that the transaction will result in approximately 95% of its earnings coming from regulated U.S. utilities, significantly reducing the need for future equity issuances to support capital improvements.

This sale marks a pivotal shift in Sempra's strategic focus, allowing the company to concentrate on its core utility operations while simultaneously bolstering its financial strength. The transaction is expected to enhance Sempra's ability to invest in its regulated utility businesses, which are crucial for growth in the evolving energy landscape. Analysts are closely watching how this move will impact Sempra's long-term growth trajectory and market positioning.

Investors are encouraged by the news, as the stock's upward movement reflects confidence in Sempra's strategic direction. The collaboration with KKR, a well-established player in the private equity space, could open new avenues for future investments and operational efficiencies. As Sempra continues to navigate the complexities of the energy market, this transaction may serve as a catalyst for further growth and innovation in the utility sector.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 239 words

Sat May 9, 2026: Sempra Targets 11% Utility Rate Base Growth by 2030

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company, is embarking on a strategic simplification of its business model to prioritize utility operations. This shift is expected to drive an 11% annual rate base growth through the year 2030, as announced on 11th May 2026.

A key factor contributing to Sempra's recent performance is the Oncor base rate review settlement. This settlement establishes a higher authorized equity layer of 43.5% and a return on equity (ROE) of 9.75%, which aligns rates more closely with the current cost environment, thereby enhancing profitability.

Texas has emerged as a focal point for infrastructure development, driven by surging demand from artificial intelligence and data centers. In response, Sempra has initiated a record $65 billion capital plan, with a significant portion of the rate base expected to be concentrated in Texas by 2030, positioning the company favorably for future growth.

To support this ambitious capital deployment, operational modernization at Oncor is underway, which includes diversifying supply chains. Sempra has secured labor and materials through 2028 to mitigate execution risks, ensuring that the company remains on track with its development goals.

In addition, Sempra Infrastructure is poised for significant near-term revenue contributions. The first production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) at ECA Phase 1 is scheduled for next month, with substantial completion anticipated by this summer. Furthermore, management has identified approximately $9 billion in incremental capital opportunities, primarily in Texas, driven by the rapid growth of large-load customers.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 258 words

Tue May 5, 2026: Sempra Strengthens Board with Jennifer M. Kirk Appointment

Sempra has announced the appointment of Jennifer M. Kirk to its board of directors, effective 20th June 2024. This strategic move comes as the company aims to enhance its governance and oversight, particularly in financial disciplines and risk management.

Kirk, who currently serves as the global controller and chief accounting officer at Medtronic plc, brings over two decades of experience in the energy sector. Her extensive background in finance and capital market activities will be instrumental in guiding the company's future strategies. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026 at 18:23:07.706818, highlighting the company's commitment to board refreshment and diversity.

Jeffrey W. Martin, the chairman and CEO, emphasized the importance of diverse perspectives in the boardroom, stating that Kirk's accounting and energy expertise will greatly benefit the company as it strives to solidify its position as North America's premier energy infrastructure provider. Kirk will also serve on the audit committee and the safety, sustainability, and technology committee.

Before her tenure at Medtronic, Kirk spent over 20 years at Occidental Petroleum Corporation, where she held various senior roles including senior vice president of integration and value capture. Her leadership experience in a major energy company aligns well with Sempra's objectives of operational excellence and sustainable practices.

With Kirk's appointment, Sempra's board now consists of ten directors, three of whom have been elected in the last five years, indicating a proactive approach to governance. The company continues to be recognized for its commitment to sustainability and high-performance culture, ensuring that it remains a leader in the energy infrastructure space.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Uptrend

Price/Moving Average Price of 1.03:

- The Price/MAP 200 for Sempra is 1.03. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $97.23 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $90.64, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $90.64, a third bullish indicator.

Past Quarter:

- In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high seven times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

2.2 Other Bullish Signals

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.6. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 21.3% in the last 3 years. This is considered superior and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 9.3% is better than sector average of 5.6%. This is computed as net profit margin of 26.5% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.14 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 2.5.

This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $10.7 billion and Created Market Value of $38.9 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $4.62.

But does not meet the following 5 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-S553-S8GP-0110-00000-00Can Sempra (NYSE:SRE), continue its gains?2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisCan Sempra (NYSE:SRE), continue its gains?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN DIEGO, Tuesday, May 12 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Sempra has shown some strength in recent days. Sempra (NYSE:SRE), the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market cap, increased 0.6% during its previous trading session to close at $93.40.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is an American energy infrastructure company specializing in the development and operation of energy networks. The company is engaged in the construction and management of electricity grids and natural gas pipelines, offering reliable energy delivery services through its extensive network. Sempra's strategic investments in renewable energy projects and innovative technology solutions highlight its commitment to sustainability. With a robust pipeline of projects focused on clean energy transition, Sempra leverages its large-scale ecosystem and technological leadership. Key competitive advantages include its expansive supply chain and ability to innovate in the energy sector.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 341 words

Sun May 10, 2026: Sempra Shares Climb Following $10 Billion Infrastructure Stake Sale

Sempra's stock price surged nearly 5% on the New York Stock Exchange following the announcement of a significant divestiture. On 10th May 2026, the San Diego-based utility holding company revealed it would sell a 45% interest in its infrastructure business to private equity firm KKR for $10 billion. This strategic move is aimed at simplifying Sempra's business model and enhancing its financial position.

The infrastructure unit being sold includes operations related to liquefied natural gas terminals, gas pipelines, and renewable energy facilities such as solar and wind generation sites. Jeffrey W. Martin, Sempra's chairman and CEO, emphasized that the transaction is designed to streamline operations and fund robust utility growth in key markets like Texas and California. He stated that this sale underscores Sempra's commitment to a long-term partnership with KKR, aligning their vision to strengthen America's global leadership in LNG exports.

Upon completion of the deal, a KKR-led consortium will hold a 65% equity stake in Sempra Infrastructure Partners, while Sempra will maintain a 25% interest and the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority will acquire the remaining 10%. Sempra indicated that the transaction will result in approximately 95% of its earnings coming from regulated U.S. utilities, significantly reducing the need for future equity issuances to support capital improvements.

This sale marks a pivotal shift in Sempra's strategic focus, allowing the company to concentrate on its core utility operations while simultaneously bolstering its financial strength. The transaction is expected to enhance Sempra's ability to invest in its regulated utility businesses, which are crucial for growth in the evolving energy landscape. Analysts are closely watching how this move will impact Sempra's long-term growth trajectory and market positioning.

Investors are encouraged by the news, as the stock's upward movement reflects confidence in Sempra's strategic direction. The collaboration with KKR, a well-established player in the private equity space, could open new avenues for future investments and operational efficiencies. As Sempra continues to navigate the complexities of the energy market, this transaction may serve as a catalyst for further growth and innovation in the utility sector.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 239 words

Sat May 9, 2026: Sempra Targets 11% Utility Rate Base Growth by 2030

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company, is embarking on a strategic simplification of its business model to prioritize utility operations. This shift is expected to drive an 11% annual rate base growth through the year 2030, as announced on 11th May 2026.

A key factor contributing to Sempra's recent performance is the Oncor base rate review settlement. This settlement establishes a higher authorized equity layer of 43.5% and a return on equity (ROE) of 9.75%, which aligns rates more closely with the current cost environment, thereby enhancing profitability.

Texas has emerged as a focal point for infrastructure development, driven by surging demand from artificial intelligence and data centers. In response, Sempra has initiated a record $65 billion capital plan, with a significant portion of the rate base expected to be concentrated in Texas by 2030, positioning the company favorably for future growth.

To support this ambitious capital deployment, operational modernization at Oncor is underway, which includes diversifying supply chains. Sempra has secured labor and materials through 2028 to mitigate execution risks, ensuring that the company remains on track with its development goals.

In addition, Sempra Infrastructure is poised for significant near-term revenue contributions. The first production of liquefied natural gas (LNG) at ECA Phase 1 is scheduled for next month, with substantial completion anticipated by this summer. Furthermore, management has identified approximately $9 billion in incremental capital opportunities, primarily in Texas, driven by the rapid growth of large-load customers.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 258 words

Tue May 5, 2026: Sempra Strengthens Board with Jennifer M. Kirk Appointment

Sempra has announced the appointment of Jennifer M. Kirk to its board of directors, effective 20th June 2024. This strategic move comes as the company aims to enhance its governance and oversight, particularly in financial disciplines and risk management.

Kirk, who currently serves as the global controller and chief accounting officer at Medtronic plc, brings over two decades of experience in the energy sector. Her extensive background in finance and capital market activities will be instrumental in guiding the company's future strategies. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026 at 18:23:07.706818, highlighting the company's commitment to board refreshment and diversity.

Jeffrey W. Martin, the chairman and CEO, emphasized the importance of diverse perspectives in the boardroom, stating that Kirk's accounting and energy expertise will greatly benefit the company as it strives to solidify its position as North America's premier energy infrastructure provider. Kirk will also serve on the audit committee and the safety, sustainability, and technology committee.

Before her tenure at Medtronic, Kirk spent over 20 years at Occidental Petroleum Corporation, where she held various senior roles including senior vice president of integration and value capture. Her leadership experience in a major energy company aligns well with Sempra's objectives of operational excellence and sustainable practices.

With Kirk's appointment, Sempra's board now consists of ten directors, three of whom have been elected in the last five years, indicating a proactive approach to governance. The company continues to be recognized for its commitment to sustainability and high-performance culture, ensuring that it remains a leader in the energy infrastructure space.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Sempra, a leading energy infrastructure company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: SRE), is a Fortune 500 holding entity headquartered in San Diego, California. The company's core business revolves around the development, operation, and investment in energy infrastructure, including natural gas utilities, pipelines, storage facilities, and electric services across North America. Through subsidiaries like San Diego Gas & Electric and Southern California Gas Company, Sempra delivers reliable energy to millions of customers, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and operational efficiency. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy projects and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports, driven by increasing demand for clean energy solutions and global market expansion. For instance, Sempra's investments in wind, solar, and battery storage, as well as its LNG facilities in Mexico and the U.S., have fueled significant growth, positioning the company as a key player in the energy transition. With a strong focus on innovation and strategic partnerships, Sempra continues to enhance its It is the NYSE's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Sempra (NYSE:SRE) is strategically positioned as a powerhouse in the energy sector, leveraging a diversified business model focused on regulated utility rate increase dynamics and robust investments in energy infrastructure. As a key player, Sempra's comprehensive base rate approval, specifically the Oncor base rate review approval, strengthens its influence in the Texas electric utility base rate landscape. The company's operations, encompassing electric transmission and distribution services and natural gas pipelines and storage, showcase its commitment to energy infrastructure growth. Significant grid modernization at Oncor, paired with reliable utility capital expenditures (capex), underscores Sempra's proactive approach in enhancing transmission and distribution investment. Additionally, renewable energy projects Sempra, along with battery storage projects Sempra, highlight its dedication to sustainable energy solutions. With competitive strengths in Sempra regulated utilities (SoCalGas SDG&E Oncor) and a focus on regulated rate base expansion, Sempra offers promising avenues for Sempra investor earnings growth amid regulatory approvals and utility revenue requirement approval.

value.Title: Eni SpA Strengthens Graphite Supply Chain with $70 Million NMG Investment

urn:contentItem:6JGB-3VX3-RV59-41RP-00000-00Eni SpA Strengthens Graphite Supply Chain with $70 Million NMG Investment2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisEni SpA Strengthens Graphite Supply Chain with $70 Million NMG Investment

DAILY ADR AND DEPOSITORY RECEIPT REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

01 May 2026 09:47 EDT

Eni S.p.A. has announced a significant equity investment of $70 million in Canadian company Nouveau Monde Graphite Inc. (NMG), aimed at strengthening its graphite supply chain. This announcement was made on 1st May 2026, and signals Eni's commitment to diversifying its supply networks in the critical minerals sector.

The investment is part of a broader $297 million fundraising initiative that includes contributions from major Canadian institutional investors such as the Canada Growth Fund and Investissement Qu bec, as well as public funding. Upon completion of the transaction, Eni will hold approximately 11.5% of NMG's equity and will secure a seat on its board, enabling a more strategic partnership between the two firms.

This collaboration will provide Eni with exclusive supply agreements for graphite and active anode material, which are vital for the production of lithium-ion batteries. This aligns with Eni's strategy to enhance its supply chain while leveraging its technological expertise, particularly for its Brindisi-based gigafactory in Italy, which focuses on stationary lithium-ion battery production.

In turn, NMG will utilize the funds to advance the development of its Matawinie mine, known for producing high-quality natural graphite. This material is not only critical for lithium-ion batteries but also for various high-margin industrial applications. The raw graphite extracted from the Matawinie mine will be processed at NMG’s B cancour Battery Material Plant in Quebec, ensuring a robust supply chain for battery-ready materials.

Currently, Eni holds a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), while NMG is rated Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). Other notable stocks in the energy sector include Chevron Corporation and BP plc, both of which also have a Zacks Rank #1. With the price of West Texas Intermediate crude trading above $100 per barrel, Eni's strategic investments reflect a proactive approach to capitalize on favorable market conditions.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 ENI S.P.A. ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Eni S.p.A. Activities

Activities

Eni SpA, an Italian multinational energy company, is a leading integrated player in the global energy sector. Its core business activities encompass the exploration, production, and marketing of oil and natural gas, as well as refining, chemicals, and power generation. The company operates across the entire energy value chain, including renewable energy projects and infrastructure development. In recent years, Eni's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as solar and wind power, which are expanding rapidly to align with global decarbonization efforts. Additionally, the natural gas business is experiencing significant growth due to increasing demand for lower-emission fuels as a bridge to a sustainable future. Eni continues to invest in innovation, including bio-fuels and carbon capture technologies, to enhance operational efficiency and support the energy transition while maintaining a strong focus on environmental stewardship. It is the International Depository Receipt (IDR) market's 4th largest Oil & gas company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JK4-0TW3-S3VK-N4NX-00000-00Energy Stocks To Follow Today - May 12th2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisEnergy Stocks To Follow Today - May 12th

Tesla, IREN, and Coterra Energy are the three Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, refining, transporting, or supplying energy, such as oil, natural gas, coal, and renewable power. For stock market investors, this sector includes businesses like major oil producers, pipeline operators, utility companies, and clean energy firms, and their performance is often influenced by commodity prices, regulation, and global demand for energy. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Energy stocks within the last several days.

Tesla TSLA

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, and energy generation and storage systems in the United States, China, and internationally. The company operates in two segments, Automotive, and Energy Generation and Storage. The Automotive segment offers electric vehicles, as well as sells automotive regulatory credits; and non-warranty after-sales vehicle, used vehicles, body shop and parts, supercharging, retail merchandise, and vehicle insurance services.

Read Our Latest Research Report on TSLA

IREN IREN

IREN Limited, formerly known as Iris Energy Limited, owns and operates bitcoin mining data centers. The company was incorporated in 2018 and is headquartered in Sydney, Australia.

Read Our Latest Research Report on IREN

Coterra Energy CTRA

Coterra Energy Inc., an independent oil and gas company, engages in the development, exploration, and production of oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids in the United States. The company's properties include the Marcellus Shale with approximately 186,000 net acres in the dry gas window of the play located in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania; Permian Basin properties with approximately 296,000 net acres located in westTexas and southeast New Mexico; and Anadarko Basin properties with approximately 182,000 net acres located in Oklahoma.

Read Our Latest Research Report on CTRA

value.Title: Tesla Strengthens China Strategy with 95% Localization Achievement

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-WT63-RSWV-80PH-00000-00Tesla Strengthens China Strategy with 95% Localization Achievement2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:03ZLexisNexisTesla Strengthens China Strategy with 95% Localization Achievement

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

13 May 2026 11:17 EDT

Tesla has always attached great importance to the Chinese market, underlining the country's efficient supply chain system and promising to introduce more intelligent products and technologies into the nation. On 13th May 2026, Grace Tao, vice-president of Tesla, stated that 'China is not only one of the world's most important new energy vehicle markets, but it also features an open business environment, a complete supply chain system, and a rich pool of highly skilled talent.'

Tao emphasized that Tesla's achievements in China were built alongside more than 400 Chinese suppliers, over 60 of which have integrated into Tesla's global supply chain. 'Currently, the localization rate of components at the Shanghai Gigafactory exceeds 95 percent,' she said. 'All this shows that the Shanghai Gigafactory is playing an increasingly important role in Tesla's global production and export network.'

The vice-president further noted that Tesla's continued presence and investment in China reflect the importance the company places on this market. As of March 2026, Tesla had opened 588 direct-operated stores across 119 cities in the Chinese mainland, enhancing its retail footprint significantly. Additionally, the company has built and put into operation more than 2,500 supercharger stations and over 12,000 supercharger connectors across the region.

Tao also highlighted Tesla's broader mission, stating, 'Tesla is not just an electric vehicle company — it is a technology company centered on AI, robotics, and energy.' Looking ahead, she indicated that the company will continue to introduce more of its intelligent products and technologies to China, aiming to contribute to building an abundant and extraordinary world.

With the Shanghai Gigafactory's high localization rate, Tesla's strategy emphasizes not only the production of electric vehicles but also a commitment to technological advancement within the Chinese market. This approach could serve as a template for other foreign companies seeking to establish a foothold in China.

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: World Oil Prices Hold Above $105 as Iran Tensions and Trump-Xi Summit Drive Volatility

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-3P43-RVRY-719F-00000-00World Oil Prices Hold Above $105 as Iran Tensions and Trump-Xi Summit Drive Volatility2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:16:04ZLexisNexisWorld Oil Prices Hold Above $105 as Iran Tensions and Trump-Xi Summit Drive Volatility

The views expressed in any and all content distributed by Newstex and its re-distributors (collectively, the "Newstex Authoritative Content") are solely those of the respective author(s) and not necessarily the views of Newstex or its re-distributors. Stories from such authors are provided "AS IS," with no warranties, and confer no rights. The material and information provided in Newstex Authoritative Content are for general information only and should not, in any respect, be relied on as professional advice. Newstex Authoritative Content is not "read and approved" before it is posted. Accordingly, neither Newstex nor its re-distributors make any claims, promises or guarantees about the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of the information contained therein or linked to from such content, nor do they take responsibility for any aspect of such content. The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Tony Jackson

May 14th, 2026 ( International Business Times Australia - Delivered by Newstex )

NEW YORK - World oil prices remained elevated above $105 per barrel on Thursday, May 14, 2026, as ongoing geopolitical risks in the Middle East, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz, continued to support the market even as investors awaited outcomes from the high-stakes summit between President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing.

Brent crude, the global benchmark, traded near $105.50 per barrel in early European trading, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI), the U.S. benchmark, hovered around $101 per barrel. Both contracts have stayed in elevated territory throughout 2026, reflecting persistent supply concerns and strong global demand despite economic uncertainties in some regions.

The primary driver remains the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint through which nearly 20% of global oil supply flows. Since military actions began in late February, shipping traffic has been severely restricted, leading to significant supply disruptions and a sharp drawdown in global inventories. Analysts at S&P Global estimate that inventories have fallen by an average of 8.5 million barrels per day in the second quarter, pushing prices higher and creating a risk premium in the market.

The Trump-Xi summit has added another layer of uncertainty and potential relief. Trump is pressing China, Iran's largest oil customer, to use its influence to help stabilize energy flows. Any positive developments from Beijing could ease pressure on prices, but analysts caution that a quick resolution to the Hormuz situation remains unlikely. "The market is pricing in prolonged disruption," said one senior energy trader. "Even optimistic scenarios suggest it will take time for flows to normalize."

U.S. production has provided some buffer. Domestic output remains near record levels, helping to offset some global tightness. However, OPEC+ members have maintained disciplined production cuts, limiting additional supply to the market. Saudi Arabia and other Gulf producers continue to prioritize price stability over volume in the current environment.

Demand remains robust despite higher prices. Strong economic activity in Asia, particularly in India and parts of Southeast Asia, has supported consumption. China's stimulus measures have helped stabilize industrial activity, though the country's overall economic recovery remains uneven. Global oil demand is projected to average around 103 million barrels per day in 2026, according to the International Energy Agency, with transportation fuels and petrochemicals driving growth.

For American consumers, the impact is noticeable at the pump. National average gasoline prices have climbed above $4.00 per gallon in many regions, adding pressure on household budgets ahead of the summer driving season. Refiners have warned that prolonged high crude costs could lead to further increases if inventory levels tighten further.

Energy companies have benefited from the elevated price environment. Major producers have reported strong earnings, with many using the windfall to pay down debt, increase dividends and invest in low-carbon technologies. Oilfield service companies have also seen renewed demand, though the focus remains on efficiency and capital discipline rather than aggressive expansion.

The Trump administration has used the situation to push for increased domestic production and streamlined permitting for energy projects. Officials argue that boosting U.S. output can help stabilize global markets and reduce reliance on foreign supplies. However, environmental groups and some Democrats in Congress have criticized the approach, calling for faster transition to renewable energy sources.

Longer-term forecasts suggest prices could remain elevated through the remainder of 2026. Standard Chartered and other banks project Brent averaging between $100 and $110 per barrel for the year, assuming the Hormuz situation persists into the third quarter. A full resolution could bring prices back toward $80-$90, but most analysts see limited downside risk in the near term.

Investors have responded with caution. Energy stocks have outperformed broader markets in 2026 but remain sensitive to any diplomatic breakthroughs or sudden supply increases. Volatility in oil futures has increased, with traders positioning for potential swings around the Trump-Xi meetings and other geopolitical developments.

The situation also highlights the interconnected nature of global energy markets. Europe, still recovering from earlier energy shocks, has increased imports of U.S. liquefied natural gas and other alternatives. Asia's reliance on Middle Eastern crude makes it particularly vulnerable to disruptions in the region.

As the Trump-Xi summit continues, any signals regarding Iran or energy cooperation could move markets significantly. For now, the combination of tight supply, strong demand and geopolitical risk keeps oil prices firmly in elevated territory, affecting everything from gasoline prices to inflation expectations worldwide.

The coming days and weeks will be critical in determining whether current levels prove sustainable or if new developments bring relief to consumers and businesses. Until then, the world remains on edge, watching both the oil markets and the diplomatic efforts in Beijing for clues about the path ahead.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

PRINCETON, Wednesday, May 13 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, NRG Energy, Inc. closed lower the previous trading day. NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG), the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market cap, sank 4.6% during its previous trading session to close at $131.08.

NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) is a diversified energy company focusing on producing and delivering electricity and related services to residential and commercial clients. Central to its operations is a portfolio of power plants and renewable energy assets, bolstered by strategic initiatives in sustainability and carbon reduction. The company actively explores innovation through its transition to cleaner energy solutions and resilient grid infrastructures. Its financial strategy includes robust capital markets activities, such as a $2.6 billion debt offering and a $900 million term loan agreement, enhancing liquidity and financial flexibility. NRG Energy maintains a competitive edge through its scale, integrated supply chain, and strategic refinancing transactions.

Mon May 11, 2026: NRG Energy's Strategic Moves Signal Growth Potential in U.S. Energy Markets

NRG Energy, a leading independent power generator, continues to expand its footprint in the U.S. retail energy and renewables sectors amid rising demand for clean energy solutions. As of 11th May 2026, the company's strategic initiatives underscore its commitment to sustainability and reliability, making it an intriguing option for investors monitoring the energy sector.

Operating from its headquarters in Houston, Texas, NRG Energy holds a significant position in the U.S. power generation and retail energy markets. The company boasts a diverse portfolio that includes natural gas, coal, oil, nuclear, solar, and battery storage facilities, serving a wide range of residential, commercial, and industrial customers. With a focus on optimizing asset performance and customer engagement, NRG leverages its substantial generation capacity of over 16 GW to maintain a competitive edge.

Retail electricity sales are the primary revenue driver for NRG Energy, accounting for approximately 70% of total revenues in 2024. The company's retail operations, which include brands like Reliant Energy, have thrived in deregulated markets such as Texas and Illinois. NRG's strategy includes long-term customer contracts and demand response programs that provide stability in earnings, especially amid fluctuating market conditions. Furthermore, the integration of renewables and battery storage into its operations aligns with the increasing demand for sustainable energy solutions.

As the U.S. power sector grapples with rising demand due to factors such as data centers and electrification, NRG Energy stands to benefit significantly. The company's strong presence in Texas positions it well to capitalize on projected capacity needs, with ERCOT forecasting an additional 50 GW required by 2030. While competitors like Vistra and Constellation Energy are also vying for market share, NRG's extensive retail capabilities and innovative storage solutions set it apart in a fragmented industry.

For U.S. investors, NRG Energy offers a compelling opportunity to engage with the backbone of America's energy infrastructure. The company's operations, particularly in high-growth regions, provide exposure to both traditional and renewable energy sectors. With a focus on maintaining reliable cash flows and strategic expansions, NRG Energy is an attractive option for those seeking long-term growth in the energy market, especially as the industry shifts towards a greener future.

Wed Apr 15, 2026: NRG Energy Secures $2.6 Billion Through Strategic Debt Offerings

HOUSTON - NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) has successfully priced a substantial $2.6 billion in debt offerings across three distinct tranches, as announced in a recent statement. This strategic move comes on the heels of the decision made on 17th April 2026 to enhance the company's liquidity and refinance existing obligations.

The offerings comprise $500 million in 4.955% senior secured first lien notes due 2031, alongside $1.05 billion in 5.875% senior unsecured notes due 2034 and another $1.05 billion in 6.125% senior unsecured notes due 2036. The new notes will be guaranteed by NRG Energy's current and future wholly-owned U.S. subsidiaries, providing additional security for investors. The secured notes are backed by a first priority security interest in collateral already pledged for NRG's existing credit agreements and senior secured notes.

As of the end of the fourth quarter of 2025, NRG Energy's total debt stood at $16.6 billion. However, the company maintains a robust current ratio of 1.64, indicating that its liquid assets comfortably exceed short-term obligations. The net proceeds from the current debt offerings will be utilized to repay a portion of outstanding borrowings under its revolving credit facility and fund the tender price for a previously announced offer by its subsidiary Lightning Power, LLC for outstanding senior secured notes. Any residual proceeds may be allocated for general corporate purposes, including the repayment of other debts.

The completion of each of the offerings is independent, with no conditions tied to the other offerings or the term loan B. The notes are being offered exclusively to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A and to non-U.S. persons outside the United States under Regulation S, with no registration under the Securities Act of 1933. This move highlights NRG Energy's commitment to optimizing its capital structure amidst a dynamic market environment.

In related news, NRG Energy's subsidiary Lightning Power has initiated a tender offer to purchase up to $1.5 billion in senior secured notes due 2032, offering $1,063.75 per $1,000 principal amount for notes tendered by 27th April 2026, including an early tender payment. Analysts have responded positively to NRG's strategic maneuvers, with Jefferies raising its price target to $199 and Wolfe Research upgrading the stock to Outperform, both citing the anticipated growth in the sector. Goldman Sachs has also reinstated coverage with a Buy rating, emphasizing the positive impact of NRG's recent acquisitions, which are expected to bolster its business profile moving forward.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

NRG Energy, Inc., a leading integrated power company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in the generation, transmission, and retail supply of electricity across the United States. The company's core activities include operating a diverse portfolio of power plants fueled by natural gas, coal, nuclear, and renewables, while also providing energy services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers. NRG emphasizes innovative retail solutions, such as demand response programs and energy management tools, to enhance efficiency and customer engagement. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support for clean energy and market demand for sustainable options. Additionally, the company's distributed energy resources, including battery storage and microgrids, are gaining momentum, driven by advancements in technology and the shift toward decentralized power systems. This strategic focus positions NRG for continued growth in a competitive and evolving energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NRG) stands as a prominent player in the integrated power generation and retail electricity industry. The company is actively engaged in the NRG Energy unsecured notes offering and NRG Energy secured notes offering, which are part of its strategic capital growth initiatives. The recent NRG notes offering is a testament to their commitment to ensuring liquidity and operational flexibility. NRG Energy notes offering, focusing on both NRG Energy bond offering and NRG bonds offering, signifies their robust financing strategy. With the NRG Energy debt offering, NRG Energy notes issuance, and NRG Energy bond issuance, they are well-positioned to meet market challenges. Furthermore, the NRG Energy debt securities offering and NRG Energy corporate bond offering cater to diverse investor interests. Announcements such as NRG Energy investor relations notes and NRG Energy press release notes offering demonstrate transparency. These activities, found in NRG Energy SEC filing notes offering and NRG Energy 8-K notes offering, ensure stakeholders remain well-informed. The company’s structured offerings, including NRG Energy prospectus unsecured notes and NRG Energy prospectus secured notes, solidify its reputation. Through NRG Energy senior unsecured notes, NRG Energy senior secured notes, and flexible options like NRG Energy fixed-rate notes offering, NRG Energy floating-rate notes offering, they engage in comprehensive financial planning. With the NRG Energy note sale announcement and NRG Energy capital markets transaction, NRG Energy underscores its adaptive policy. Their engagement in NRG Energy debt financing and analysis of NRG Energy credit rating impact showcase its strategic foresight. Being proactive with NYSE:NRG notes offering and NRG note offering news signifies market alertness. By regularly issuing NRG Energy bond prospectus and tailoring NRG Energy institutional notes offering and NRG Energy retail notes offering, NRG Energy positions itself as a compelling choice for both retail and institutional investors.

urn:contentItem:6JJW-0343-RWNK-B2HW-00000-00Head-To-Head Survey: TXNM Energy (NYSE:TXNM) vs. Ameresco (NYSE:AMRC)2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisHead-To-Head Survey: TXNM Energy (NYSE:TXNM) vs. Ameresco (NYSE:AMRC)

May 12th, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

TXNM Energy ( NYSE:TXNM - Get Free Report) and Ameresco ( NYSE:AMRC - Get Free Report) are both energy companies, but which is the better stock? We will contrast the two companies based on the strength of their institutional ownership, earnings, dividends, profitability, analyst recommendations, valuation and risk.

Profitability

This table compares TXNM Energy and Ameresco's net margins, return on equity and return on assets.

Institutional & Insider Ownership

91.7% of TXNM Energy shares are owned by institutional investors. Comparatively, 99.2% of Ameresco shares are owned by institutional investors. 1.1% of TXNM Energy shares are owned by company insiders. Comparatively, 45.0% of Ameresco shares are owned by company insiders. Strong institutional ownership is an indication that hedge funds, endowments and large money managers believe a stock will outperform the market over the long term.

Valuation & Earnings

This table compares TXNM Energy and Ameresco"s top-line revenue, earnings per share (EPS) and valuation.

TXNM Energy has higher revenue and earnings than Ameresco. TXNM Energy is trading at a lower price-to-earnings ratio than Ameresco, indicating that it is currently the more affordable of the two stocks.

Volatility & Risk

TXNM Energy has a beta of 0.16, indicating that its share price is 84% less volatile than the S&P 500. Comparatively, Ameresco has a beta of 2.55, indicating that its share price is 155% more volatile than the S&P 500.

Analyst Ratings

This is a summary of current recommendations for TXNM Energy and Ameresco, as provided by MarketBeat.com.

TXNM Energy presently has a consensus target price of $60.88, suggesting a potential upside of 2.73%. Ameresco has a consensus target price of $42.11, suggesting a potential upside of 43.82%. Given Ameresco's stronger consensus rating and higher possible upside, analysts plainly believe Ameresco is more favorable than TXNM Energy.

About TXNM Energy

( Get Free Report)

TXNM Energy, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides electricity and electric services in the United States. It operates through Public Service Company of New Mexico (PNM) and Texas-New Mexico Power Company (TNMP) segments. The PNM segment engages in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. The segment owns and leases communications, office and other equipment, office space, vehicles, and real estate. It generates electricity using coal, natural gas and oil, and nuclear fuel and waste, as well as solar, wind, geothermal, and battery storage energy sources. The TNMP segment provides regulated transmission and distribution services. The segment also owns and leases vehicles, service facilities, and office locations throughout its service territory. The company serves residential, commercial, and industrial customers and end-users of electricity in New Mexico and Texas. The company was formerly known as PNM Resources, Inc and changed its name to TXNM Energy, Inc. in August 2024. TXNM Energy, Inc. was founded in 1882 and is based in Albuquerque, New Mexico.

About Ameresco

( Get Free Report)

Ameresco, Inc., a clean technology integrator, provides a portfolio of energy efficiency and renewable energy supply solutions in the United States, Canada, Europe, and internationally. It operates through U.S. Regions, U.S. Federal, Canada, Europe, Alternative Fuels, and All Other segments. The company offers energy efficiency, infrastructure upgrades, energy security and resilience, asset sustainability, and renewable energy solutions for businesses and organizations. It designs, develops, engineers, and installs projects that reduce the energy, as well as operations and maintenance (O&M) costs of its customers' facilities; and projects primarily include various measures customized for the facility and designed to enhance the efficiency of building systems, such as heating, ventilation, cooling, and lighting systems. In addition, the company offers renewable energy solutions and services, such as the construction of small-scale plants that the company owns or develops for customers that produce electricity, gas, heat, or cooling from renewable sources of energy and O&M services; and electricity, processed renewable gas fuel, and heat or cooling produced from renewable sources of energy. Further, the company sells photovoltaic (PV) solar energy products and systems, as well as provides consulting, and enterprise energy management services; and operates wind farms. It serves the federal, state, local governments, healthcare and educational institutions, airports, public housing authorities and public universities, municipal utilities, and commercial and industrial customers. As of December 31, 2023, the company owned and operated 185 small-scale renewable energy plants and solar PV installations. Ameresco, Inc. was incorporated in 2000 and is headquartered in Framingham, Massachusetts.
